name: S. Cristo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 D1 S. Cristo

name: Cluster of 9 mausolea
description: South of modern Kherba Sidi el Ahmar in Algeria (BAtlas 29 G1 unlabeled)

name: Tumuli on Djebel Oulad Ali and Djebel Djerab
description: Multiple tumuli located on the ridges of the Djebel Oulad Ali and Djebel Djerab in Algeria, noted by Gsell (AAA, whence BAtlas).

name: Groups of bazinas (tumuli)
description: Tombs identified in AAA to the southeast of modern Haraza (حرازة) in Algeria's Kabylia province as mapped on BAtlas 30 H4 and 31 A4.

name: Cemetery at Ain bou Merzoug
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 F4 Ain bou Merzoug

name: Necropolis and Mortuary Complex of Pepi I
description: Located in south Saqqara, this monument and associated necropolis date to the Sixth Dynasty of ancient Egypt (Dynasty VI).

name: Bagneux Megalith
description: A megalithic dolmen located in Saumur, France. This is one of the largest dolmens in France.

name: Kercado tumulus
description: A dolmen that is still covered by its original cairn, the Kercado tumulus was built ca. 4600 B.C. and remained in use for approximately 3,000 years.

name: Investiture relief of Ardashir I
description: A rock-cut relief depicting the investiture of Ardashir I, ca. A.D. 226-242. This relief is the earliest Sasanian monument at Naqš-e Rustam.

name: Hochdorf Chieftain's Grave
description: Tumulus covering a rich Celtic burial of ca. 530 BC.

name: Abusir Pharos tomb
description: A tomb that seems to imitate the famous lighthouse of Alexandria.

name: Kapilikaya Rock Tomb
description: A rock-cut tomb dated to the second century BCE.

name: Poulnabrone dolmen
description: The Poulnabrone dolmen is a portal tomb in County Clare, Ireland, dating between 4200 and 2900 BCE.

name: Tomb of Marcus Tullius
description: The schola-type tomb of one Marcus Tullius is located outside the Porta Stabia of Pompeii. The decedent is thought to have been the patron of the Aedes For­tunae Augustae and was three times duovir and tribunus militum a populo.

name: Monte Abatone necropolis, Caere
description: A necropolis of ancient Caere with particularly important evidence of funerary assemblages showing the influence of the Orientalizing period.

name: Northern necropolis at Apulum
description: Cemetery site near the town of Apulum containing over a thousand burials dated to the late 1st-mid-3rd century CE.

name: Kudachurt
description: Burial site in the Northern Caucusus containing 130 burials dated to the Middle-Late Bronze Age (2200-1650 BCE).

name: Necropoli Romana
description: A Roman necropolis at Brixia.

name: Tumulus de Péré
description: The Péré tumulus, or Pairé tumulus, is a Neolithic tumulus that dates from the fifth millennium B.C. The site is located in Prissé-la-Charrière, in Deux-Sèvres, in New Aquitaine, France. It is composed of three main megalithic structures, built at different times.

name: Necròpolis fenicia de Jardin
description: A Punic necropolis located in Los Toscanos, Spain, that was in use from the sixth to the fourth centuries BCE.

name: Kasta tomb
description: A tumulus of Macedonian or Roman date located in the hinterland of Amphipolis. The tomb was discovered in 2012 and first entered in 2014.

name: Sepulcrum Hirtii
description: The tomb of Aulus Hirtius, consul in 43 B.C., was discovered beneath the northwest corner of the Palazzo della Cancelleria in Rome in 1938.

name: Grave Circle B
description: Grave Circle B is a burial complex of shaft and cist graves located 130 meters west of the Lion Gate at Mycenae. It was used for the burials of elite families between approximately 1625 and 1550 BC.

name: Tumulo Etrusco del Molinello (Asciano)
description: An Etruscan tumulus-type tomb at Asciano with artifactual evidence dating to the Orientalizing period.

name: Necropoli di Celle
description: Extensive areas of rock-cut tombs in the vicinity of Falerii Veteres (modern Civita Castellana in Italy)

name: Lamā Cemetery
description: Cemetery site near Lama village, Iran with almost 60 graves dating to the late 2nd-early 1st millennium CE. Most of the graves were destroyed by bulldozing in the area for a road in December 1999.

name: Mausoleum of M. Coelius Vinicianus
description: A mid-first century B.C. tomb at Tusculum.

name: Tholos tomb of Apodoulou
description: Mycenaean tholos-type tomb located near Apodoulou in Crete.

name: Mausoleum of Clesippus Geganius
description: The ruins of a first century B.C. Roman tomb along the Via Appia at Mesa di Pontinia.

name: Via Olevano Romano columbarium
description: A late republican to early imperial columbarium along the Via Praenestina. The columbarium is of rectangular plan and was last used during the early Flavian period.

name: Mausoleo della Conocchia
description: A Roman mausoleum dating most likely to the second century.

name: Karkabhat
description: Megalithic site in Chhattisgarh, India containing over 800 burials and several stone monuments dated to the first millennium BCE.

name: Sidorovka Burial Ground
description: Burial ground in modern Omsk Oblast, Russia from the Sargat Culture with five burial mounds.

name: Temple of Amenhotep, son of Hapu
description: A mortuary temple located in the Theban Necropolis that was dedicated to one Amenhotep, son of Hapu, an official in the court of the 18th Dynasty pharaoh Amenhotep III. Numerous roles are attributed to Amenhotep, son of Hapu, including those of priest, scribe, and architect. Likely he received special dispensation for the construction of the mortuary temple.

name: Yortan
description: Cemetery in modern Turkey's Manisa province with remains from the Early Bronze Age. This is the typesite for the Yortan archaeological culture.

name: Tushqah
description: Nubian Cemetery site along the Nile in use in the Middle Kingdom and the Second Intermediate Period. The site was inundated by the creation of the Aswan Dam.

name: Prokhorovka
description: Burial ground with four mounds from the early Sarmatian period. The site is located near the modern town Prokhorovka in Orenberg Oblast, Russia.

name: La Brèche (necropolis)
description: A large necropolis excavated under rescue conditions in the 20th century. Grave goods indicated a long period of use, stretching from as early as the third century CE to as late as the sixteenth. The site is located in the French commune of Laudun-l'Ardoise, near the confluences of the Tave, Céze, and Rhône rivers.

name: Font-du-Buis
description: An ancient necropolis documented during rescue excavation in the early 1970s at a location known as Font-du-Buis or La Fontaine du Buis between Saze and Avingon in Gard, France.

name: Lansargues
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 C2 Lansargues

name: Rognes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E2 Rognes

name: St-Andéol
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E1 St-Andéol

name: St-Julien
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A3 St-Julien

name: Tavşan Tepe Tümülüsü
description: The Tavşan Tepe Tümülüsü is a tumulus at Pergamon included in the UNESCO World Heritage site "Pergamon and its Multi-Layered Cultural Landscape".

name: Via Statilia tombs
description: Late Republican chamber tombs (ca. 100 B.C.) located at the intersection of the modern Via Statilia and Via di Santa Croce in Rome.

name: Pyramids of Meroe
description: The pyramid tombs of the kings of the Kushite kingdom.

name: Chiprana
description: A Roman mausoleum in Chiprana, Spain, known commonly as the mausoleum of the Fabii.

name: Tomba della Medusa (Roma)
description: A Roman chamber tomb now located on the grounds of the Policlinico Umberto I hospital in Rome.

name: Ain Aata
description: Sanctuary of the Roman period

name: Phourni
description: A Minoan necropolis located near Mount Juktas that was in use from the Early Minoan II to Late Minoan IIIC periods.

name: Necropoli Orti della Maddalena
description: A Hellenistic and Roman period necropolis located in Messina, Sicily, in the area now referred to as "Orti della Maddalena".

name: Poggio Gaiella
description: An Etruscan tumulus of the fifth century BCE, the tumulus of Poggio Gaiella has been considered by some scholars to be the tomb of Porsenna.

name: Melone del Sodo
description: Etruscan tumulus-type tombs of the seventh through fourth centuries B.C. located near Cortona, Italy, in località Il Mulino.

name: Vrysi tou Barba
description: One of the few known coastal Early and Middle Bronze Age sites in Cyprus. This site is a cemetery located in modern Lapithos, Cyprus.

name: Poggio Gramignano Roman Villa
description: A Roman villa in the modern Lugnano commune of Italy's Terni province. Substantial architectural remains dating to the Roman imperial period have been excavated there, as well as a later (5th century CE?) collection of child and infant burials that gained significant media attention.

name: So-called Grotta Fragapane
description: A large and rich Early Christian necropolis on the Via Sacra in Agrigento, dated to the fourth century A.D.

name: Torraccio a Poggio Moiano
description: A Roman mausoleum of the second century A.D.

name: Phaneromeni Burials (Chiliomodi)
description: A monumental sarcophagus of archaic date was excavated in situ in 1984 at a place known today as Phaneromeni in the eastern part of modern Chiliomodi's (Greece) village territory. Its location provides evidence for the urban extent of ancient Tenea.

name: Roman Mausoleum at Palio Scholeio (Chiliomedio)
description: A monumental Roman mausoleum, originally installed over a Hellenistic cemetery, was excavated in 2017 at a site known as "Old School" to the south of modern Chiliomedio in Greece. Its location helps indicate the extent and date of cemeteries at the ancient site of Tenea.

name: Tantalus Monu.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 Tantalus Monu.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E4 unnamed tumulus (W Moschakome)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E4 unnamed tumulus (cluster W *Panda)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F3 unnamed tumulus (NE Apollonis)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 unnamed tumulus (S Apollonis)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 unnamed tumulus (cluster ESE Thyateira)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 unnamed tumulus (3 around Gygaia L.)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 unnamed tumulus (6 clusters in Korou Pedion)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F5 unnamed tumulus (E Boukolion)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 G4 unnamed tumulus (cluster SW *Odon)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 G4 unnamed tumulus (4 clusters in Sardianon Pedion/Katakekaumene)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 G5 unnamed tumulus (3 near Koloe)

name: Funerary chapel of Amenirdis I
description: Funerary chapel of the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Amenirdis I, daughter of King Kashta.

name: Hypogeum at Torre del Giudizio
description: A Roman hypogeum-type tomb in the via Teodora was reused in the construction of the tower known as Torre del Giudizio.

name: Tomb of Caius Cartilius Poplicola
description: The Tomb of Caius Cartilius Poplicola, dating to ca. 30-20 B.C., is a monumental tomb outside the Porta Marina of Ostia. Poplicola was a prominent citizen of Ostia.

name: Catacombs of Trypiti
description: The rock-cut Catacombs of Milos date from the first to fifth centuries.

name: Pyramid of Cestius
description: An ancient pyramid-shaped tomb built for Caius Cestius (ca. 18-12 BC), a magistrate and member of the Septemviri Epulonum. The tomb stands at the fork of the ancient Via Ostiensis and was later incorporated into the city's fortifications.

name: Sep. Eurysacis
description: The tomb of a baker, M. Vergilius Eurysaces, built at the point where the via Labicana and the via Praenestina exit from Rome's walls to the east. The tomb dates to the later Republican period (ca. 50-20 B.C.).

name: Mausoleum of Galba
description: The so-called mausoleum of Galba is a Roman period tomb located in Monte San Biagio Latium, Italy.

name: Catacomb of San Zotico
description: A Christian catacomb located at the tenth mile of the ancient Via Labicana.

name: Khar gov'
description: Burial site in western Mongolia containing a stone cairn and several deer stone monuments, dated to the 8th-7th centuries BCE.

name: Necropolis of Ponte Messato
description: A pre-Roman and Roman period necropolis on the outskirts of Teramo.

name: Keston Roman Mausoleum
description: A circular Roman mausoleum was located at the Keston Roman villa. It dates to the third century CE and is located in a cemetery to the north of the villa.

name: Dinkha Tepe
description: The site of Dinkha Tepe is located in the Ushnu valley of Iranian Azerbaijan. Sir Aurel Stein initially identified second-millennium BCE funereal deposits there. The University of Pennsylvania Museum, under Robert H. Dyson, jr. and Oscar White Muscarella, carried out further excavations in the late 1960s.

name: Verbovskiĭ Mogil'nik
description: A burial mound dated to the fourth-third centuries BCE located northwest of the city of Stavropol'.

name: Tomb of Publius Vesonius Phileros
description: An aedicula tomb built near the Porta Nocera ca. A.D. 25-50 by Publius Vesonius Phileros for himself, his patroness Vesonia, and his friend Marcus Orfellius Faustus. A later added inscription shows a falling out between the two friends.

name: Carranque
description: A palatial complex that includes a villa originally built during the fourth century, Carranque also has significant Visigothic and Christian phases.

name: Unnamed Roman tomb
description: An unnamed Roman tomb that is located near Casale Ghella.

name: İskilip rock tomb
description: An ancient rock tomb located near modern İskilip in Turkey.

name: Royal Kurgan
description: The so-called Royal Kurgan or Tsarskiy Kurgan dates to the fourth century BCE. The burial mound is located in present-day Kerch.

name: Northern Stelae Field
description: An expansive area of funereal monuments at Aksum in Ethiopia, built between the third and fourth centuries A.D.

name: Teniky
description: An archaeological site in the modern Parc National de l'Isalo in southern Madagascar. The site comprises rock-cut terraces, niches, walls, and other features in a style unique in the region. Radiocarbon dating of charcoal and stylistic dating of imported ceramics place activity at the site to a period spanning the 10th - 12th centuries CE. The excavators tentatively interpret the site as part of a former necropolis made by settlers with Zoroastrian origins.

name: Tomba del Timpano
description: A tomb of the Hellenistic period located close to a Graeco-Siceliote center.

name: Formello villa and mausoleum
description: A Roman villa in the territory of Blera of which a cistern and a mausoleum remain.

name: Ponte Rotto necropolis
description: The Ponte Rotto necropolis is associated with the Etruscan city of Vulci.

name: Tagisken South
description: Saka necropolis with burials from the 7th-5th century BCE on the Tagisken plateau.

name: Tomb 3 (Tombs of the Kings)
description: Tomb 3 is a rock-cut tomb with a peristyle courtyard in the "Tombs of the Kings" necropolis of Nea Paphos.

name: Douvanli
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Douvanli

name: Ak-Mechet'
description: A major Scythian kurgan of the 5th c. BC partially excavated in 1885.

name: Oguz
description: The Oguz Kurgan is the site of an elite Scythian burial dating to the fourth century BCE.

name: Banditaccia necropolis, Caere
description: The Etruscan necropolis of Caere, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

name: Monterozzi necropolis
description: The Monterozzi necropolis is an Etruscan necropolis located to the east of Tarquinia. The necropolis contains about 6,000 graves.

name: Selvicciola necropolis
description: A prehistoric (Eneolithic) necropolis in mid-Tyrrhenian Italy with activity beginning in the early fourth millennium BCE. The Selvicciola necropolis is located in the valley of the Fiora River.

name: Tomb of Trebius Iustus
description: A Late Antique hypogeum tomb discovered in 1911 and dedicated to Trebius Iustus and Honoratia Severina.

name: Patronus tomb
description: A painted tomb located near the Porta Latina in Rome. The tomb's owner, Patronus, is identified as a Greek physician of the first century CE.

name: Beit She’arim
description: Beit She'arim National Park is the site of a large number of ancient rock-cut Jewish tombs, dating to the second century AD. The necropolis was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage site in 2015.

name: Zorats Karer
description: Zorats Karer is a prehistoric archaeological site of the Middle Bronze and Iron Ages located in Armenia. Over 200 rock-cut tombs and monuments have been discovered at the site.

name: West Kennet Long Barrow
description: The West Kennet Long Barrow is a chambered long barrow located near the village of Avebury in south-western England. It dates to Britain's Early Neolithic period.

name: La Dogana
description: A Roman tomb at Centuripe known as "La Dogana".

name: heroon of Aeneas
description: A tumulus at Lavinium located near the Latin federal sanctuary that has been associated with the tomb of Aeneas and/or a heroön dedicated to him.

name: Tomb of the Volumnii and Palazzone necropolis
description: A hypogeum-type tomb located in Ponte San Giovanni, a suburb of Perugia. The tomb is generally dated to the third century B.C.

name: Tumek-Kichidzhik
description: Neolithic burial site located near the eastern bank of the Sarygamysh Lake in modern Turkmenistan, containing remains dated to the 5th-4th millennia BCE.

name: Via Ostiensis necropolis
description: A necropolis at Ostia that began during the second century B.C. on the south side of the Via Ostiensis.

name: Necropoli Giambertoni
description: A Hellenistic and Roman necropolis at Agrigento (second century BC to the third century AD) that includes the Tomb of Theron.

name: Mu-Sharet Burial Ground
description: Burial ground with 25 graves in 11 mounds located 10km Northeast of Manych, Kalmykia. These graves are largely from the Bronze Age and early Copper Age with some early Iron Age and Medieval graves also present.

name: Mausoleum of the Julii
description: The mausoleum of the three Julii brothers, ca. 40 B.C.

name: Hypogeum of Ħal-Saflieni (Malta)
description: A prehistoric, subterranean temple; named as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1980.

name: Lamiospito Tholos Tomb, Dimini
description: Late Helladic III B tholos tomb on the west foot of the Neolithic hill settlement.

name: Kataret es-Samra
description: An archaeological site containing Bronze Age burials located near the confluence of the Wadi Zarqa and the Jordan River.

props.ancientName: Kataret es-Samra, Qataret es-Samra, Katarit es-Samra

name: Isis Tomb
description: Now lost, the so-called "Isis Tomb" was excavated at Vulci in the 19th century. Based on finds evidence, the tomb is traditionally dated ca. 625 to 550 B.C. Its traditionally assigned name is likely a misnomer, based on a contemporary identification of a bronze bust as representing the Egyptian goddess Isis. The subsequent back-filling of the tomb and uncareful recording at the time have caused the tomb's location to be lost to us.

name: Tomba del Sileno
description: The Tomba del Sileno is an Etruscan tomb at the Sovana Archaeological Park in the province of Grosseto. Dating from the third century BCE, the tomb derives its name from a bas-relief resembling the head of a Silen.

name: Tagisken North
description: Late Bronze-early Iron Age necropolis on the Tagisken plateau used throughout the first half of the first millennium BCE.

name: Catacombs of San Senatore
description: A Christian catacomb located at the fifteenth mile of the Via Appia in use from the late third century A.D. until the Middle Ages.

name: Pantanello necropolis
description: Burial ground located 3.5 km west of Metaponto containing tombs dated from 600-280 BCE.

name: Portone necropolis at Volaterrae
description: The so-called Portone necropolis is a major Hellenistic necropolis outside the walls of Volaterrae.

name: Horvat Shema'
description: Ancient synagogue site and burial place of Rabbi Shamai just south of Meron, Israel. The site was occupied in the early 1st century BCE, again from the late 2nd to the late 3rd, from the early 4th century to the mid-7th century, from this point until the mid-9th century, and finally from the mid-12th century until the late-13th century.

name: Tomba Lattanzi, Norchia
description: A Hellenistic, rock-cut tomb at Norchia. The site is located within the modern municipal boundaries of Viterbo in Lazio, Italy.

name: Aktash Burial Ground
description: Burial site on Lake Aktash on the Crimean peninsula made up of at least 12 mounds from the Scythian, Taurian, and Greek cultures.

name: Columbarium of the freedmen of Augustus
description: An early imperial columbarium tomb on the Via Appia.

name: Tomba del Timpano
description: A rock-cut tomb of the sixth and fifth centuries B.C.

name: Tomb of Thutmes I and Hatshepsut (KV20)
description: KV20 may be the earliest tomb cut in the Valley of the Kings. It was first the tomb of Thutmose I, although he was later re-interred in KV38. KV20 was adapted to accommodate both her and her father.

name: Tomb of Theron
description: Theron was a fifth century B.C. tyrant of Akragas. A tomb located near the Porta Aurea of the city is traditionally referred to as being that of the tyrant.

name: Pian del Vescovo
description: The Pian del Vescovo is the western necropolis of Blera and perhaps the most extensive. A variety of tomb types dating to the seventh and sixth centuries BCE are attested.

name: Etruscan necropolis of Terrone
description: A rock-cut Etruscan necropolis located at Blera.

name: Roman mausoleum at Bou Arada
description: The mausoleum of Caius Iulius Augustus Felix in Bou Arada, Tunisia.

name: Tomba del Duce, Vetulonia
description: An elite tomb at Vetulonia dating to the late eighth century BCE. Among the tomb's occupants was one Rachu Kakanas. The artifact assemblage includes numerous elite objects, among them a bronze boat that is attributed to the Nuragic culture of Sardinia.

name: Grotta Porcina tumulus
description: A rock-cut tumulus of the first half of the sixth century B.C.

name: Mausoleum of Lucilius Peto
description: A tumulus-type tomb discovered in 1887 belonging to the military tribune M. Lucilius Paetus of the gens Lucilia.

name: Sep. Scipionum
description: A family tomb of the Cornelii Scipiones near the Porta San Sebastiano in use from the third century B.C. to the first century A.D.

name: Black Pyramid
description: The pyramid of the Middle Kingdom Pharaoh Amenemhat III.

name: Ėl'khotovskiĭ nekropolʹ
description: Burial site in North Ossetia–Alania containing 58 burials associated with the Koban culture (8th-7th centuries BCE), as well as 26 medieval burials (14th century CE).

props.ancientName: Ėlʹkhotovskiĭ nekropolʹ, Ėlʹkhotovskiĭ mogilʹnik, Ėlʹkhotovskiy nekropolʹ, Ėlʹkhotovskiy mogilʹnik

name: Tofet
description: The Punic tofet at Sulcis.

name: Pyramid of Amenemhat III
description: Pyramid and tomb of Pharaoh Amenemhat III of the Twelfth Dynasty.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 B4 no. 1 (mausoleum)

name: Mausoleum of Quintus Lollius Urbicus
description: Quintus Lollius Urbicus served as a governor of Britannia between 139 and 142. His career is detailed on a pair of inscriptions from his hometown of Tiddis. His mausoleum is located a few kilometers north of Tiddis.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 A4 unnamed unlabeled (7 tumuli W Castellum Turrense)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 D3 unnamed unlabeled (tumulus near Zimizes)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 G4 unnamed unlabeled (tumulus S Calama)

name: Necropoli fenicio-punica di Othoca
description: The Punic necropolis at Othoca in Sardinia.

name: Visigothic necropolis, Duratón
description: A Visigothic necropolis at Duratón, Spain, that was discovered in 1929.

name: Theban Tomb 320
description: Theban Tomb 320, known as the "Royal Cache," was constructed during the 18th Dynasty near Deir el-Bahari. It was used as a secondary burial site for over 50 royal and noble mummies during the New Kingdom period.

name: Salve necropolis
description: A Chalcolithic necropolis with tumulus-type tombs.

name: Cavo degli Zucchi
description: A Roman necropolis along the Via Amerina.

name: Necropolis of Porta Nola
description: An area with three tombs located outside the Porta di Nola.

name: İkiz Tepe Tümülüsü
description: İkiz Tepe Tümülüsü (also Ikili Tumuli) is a tumulus near Pergamon that is included in the UNESCO World Heritage site "Pergamon and its Multi-Layered Cultural Landscape".

name: Souskiou-Vathyrkakas Cemetery
description: Cemetery site in southwest Cyprus lying 1.5km southwest of Souskiou Village with archaeological remains from the chalcolithic period.

name: Cave of the Seven Sleepers (Ephesus)
description: A site at Ephesus that is one of several contenders in western Asia for the site of the tradition of the 'seven sleepers', individuals who sought refuge in a cave ca. 250 CE and then emerged from said cave 309 lunar years later, according to the Quran.

name: Amathus necropolis
description: The necropolis of Amathus on Cyprus.

name: Mastaba of Khentika
description: The Mastaba of Khentika at Qila ad-Babba near Balat in ad-Dakhla depression, Egypt, dates from the Sixth Dynasty of ancient Egypt. Khentika was a wealthy official and served as the governor of the Dakhla Oasis during the reign of Pharaoh Pepi II, (2284 – ca. 2216 BCE).

name: Roman necropolis at Šempeter v Savinjski Dolini
description: Šempeter v Savinjski Dolini lies along the former Roman road from Aquilea to Poetovio. The necropolis, dating to the first through third centuries A.D., may be associated with anicent Celeia.

name: Apesokari Tholos tomb A
description: Minoan tomb in the Mesara Plain in south-central Crete dated to the Early Minoan II period (ca. 2200-2050 BCE) and in use until the Middle Minoan III period (ca. 1750-1675 BCE).

name: Mastaba of Kagemni
description: The Sixth Dynasty vizier Kagemni was buried in a large limestone mastaba at Saqqara. It is the largest mastaba in the Teti cemetery. Its relief program includes well-preserved depictions of quotidian life in ancient Egypt.

name: Headless Pyramid
description: The so-called "Headless Pyramid" located at Saqqara, also known as Lepsius XXIX, has an unclear identification. It is either connected to the Fifth Dynasty pharaoh Menkauhor or pharaoh Merikare of the Tenth Dynasty.

name: Pyramid of Neferirkare
description: A pyramid at Abu Sir built for the Fifth Dynasty pharaoh Neferirkare Kakai.

name: Angelika tholos tomb (Mykonos)
description: A Mycenaean tholos tomb located on the island of Mykonos.

name: Darnaychi
description: A cemetery site in southern Tajikistan containing burials from the Vaksh culture (mid-late 3rd millennium BCE).

name: Starosel Monumental Tomb
description: A 4th century BCE tholos tomb, embedded in a tumulus known as Chetinyova Mogila (Chetinyova Mound), located northwest of the modern Bulgarian village of Starosel.

name: Thracian Tomb of Kazanlak
description: A fourth century BC Thracian tomb near the ancient city of Seuthopolis discovered in 1944 and inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1979.

name: Pyramid of Djedkare-Isesi
description: The Pyramid of Djedkare Isesi in South Saqqara was built for the Fifth Dynasty pharaoh Djedkare Isesi.

name: Pyramid of Sahure
description: A pyramid complex dating to the Fifth Dynasty of Ancient Egypt and associated with the Egyptian pharaoh Sahure.

name: Necropolis of Montessu
description: The Necropolis of Montessu is located near Villaperuccio, Sardinia. It is the site of about 40 domus de Janas tombs and dates to the third millennium B.C.

name: Tholos tomb at Marathon
description: A large Mycenaean tholos tomb at Marathon.

name: Tsepi at Marathon
description: A large Early Helladic cemetery excavated at the Leoforos Plataion near Marathon, Attiki.

name: Samnite necropolis, Saticula
description: An area of Samnite tombs near ancient Saticula.

name: Tomba della Fibula d'oro
description: A corridor-type tomb in the territory of Vetulonia dating to the Orientalizing period.

name: Larnaka
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D3 Larnaka

name: Abbiar Gabbus
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: Abul Awlaf
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: el-Areg
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: el-Fredgia
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: Gebel Mota
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: Schiatuh
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: el-Wazidi
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: Zawa
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: Tomba dei Carri
description: A mid-seventh century BCE tumulus at Populonia with a diameter of 28 meters; part of the San Cerbone necropolis.

name: Ostrusha mound
description: A Thracian tumulus located near Shipka, Bulgaria, the so-called Ostrusha mound dates to the middle of the fourth century BCE.

name: Thespian Polyandrion (Boeotia)
description: A communal tomb dated to 424 BCE that was once crowned by a lion.

name: Mausoleum Hadriani
description: Mausoleum Hadriani (now Castel Sant'Angelo) located on the right bank of the Tiber River was an imperial mausoleum built by Hadrian between 134 and 139 CE. It continued as a tomb for subsequent emperors through to Caracalla, who died in 217 CE. By 401 the tomb had become a fortress with Honorius incorporating it into the line of the Aurelianic walls. During Alaric's sacking of Rome in 410 the mausoleum was looted and the ash urns dispersed. Procopius describes the destruction of the tomb's bronze statuary in 537. By the fourteenth century, the mausoleum had become a papal fortress. At one point, a fortified corridor known as Passetto di Borgo connected the fortress to St. Peter's Basilica.

name: Hala Sultan Tekke
description: A major Late Bronze Age settlement occupied the site of Hala Sultan Tekke, which later became an important Sufi Islamic shrine associated with Umm Haram.

name: Valley of the Queens
description: A valley in Egypt where the burials of both Queens and Princes took place from the sixteenth to the eleventh centuries B.C. (Eighteenth to the Twentieth Dynasties).

name: Chervony Mayak
description: Chervony Mayak (Kherson Oblast, Ukraine) is the site of a necropolis associated with the Late Scythian culture (second and mid-third centuries CE).

name: Tomb of Caecilia Agathia
description: The Tomb of Caecilia Agathia in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis of Pompeii.

name: Tomb of Novia Amoena
description: The Tomb of Novia Amoena C. L., Lucius Iacellius Virillio, Caius Novius Lupercus G.L., Cnaeus Turranius Primus, and Cornelia Quieta in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.

name: Unnamed naiskos tomb, Fondo Pacifico
description: An unnamed naiskos tomb located in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.

name: Roman tomb (Allifae)
description: A tower-type Roman tomb located near Allifae.

name: Sotira
description: Site approximately 1 km north of the modern village of Lapithos, northern Cyprus, containing several burials from the Early and Middle Bronze Age.

name: Greppe S. Angelo necropolis
description: A rock-cut necropolis of Caere that is noted as the findspot of the Sarpedon krater of the artist Euphronios.

name: Sorbo Necropolis - Regolini-Galassi tomb
description: The "Regolini-Galassi tomb" at Caere was discovered in 1836. The well-preserved tomb and its super-elite grave assemblage dates the tomb ca. 680/676-650 BCE. The objects in the assemblage are indicative of the so-called "Orientalizing" phenomenon in Etruria.

name: Maes Howe
description: Maes Howe is a Neolithic chambered cairn and passage grave on Mainland, Orkney, Scotland.

name: Bab el-Gasous
description: The collective tomb known in modern times as "La Cachette de Bab el-Gasous" at Deir el-Bahari in Egypt was discovered in the early twentieth century and its contents (the remains and funerary equipment of 153 members of the Theban elite dating to the end of the 21st dynasty) were distributed to some 90 museums around the world. The tomb extended under the "Court of Hatshepsut" from a concealed entry located north of the east end of the court and now surrounded by a low stone wall.

name: Tomb of Cuccumella
description: An archaic tumulus-type tomb at Vulci.

name: Tomb of the Dolphin
description: An early Hellenistic painted tomb at Vulci.

name: Domus de Janas on Monte Crobu
description: A prehistoric chamber tomb of the third millennium B.C. The 'Domus de Janas' tombs are typical of the Ozieri and Beaker cultures and are found chiefly on Sardinia.

name: Library of Celsus
description: The Library of Celsus is a library and monumental tomb in Ephesus which was constructed ca. 110-120 CE.

name: Bent Pyramid
description: A pyramid built by Pharaoh Sneferu (ca. 2600 BC).

name: Red Pyramid
description: The third pyramid built by Pharaoh Sneferu.

name: Mira Lanza
description: The remains of an Archaic period structure at Mira Lanza, located in Via Luigi Pierantoni.

name: el-Lahun
description: el-Lahun was a planned 'pyramid town' that supported a royal mortuary complex and other activities associated with the Pyramid of Senusret II, which was located less than a kilometer west of the settlement.

name: Alcalar megaliths
description: A Calcolithic necropolis comprised of megalithic monuments located near Portimão, Portugal.

name: Catacomb of Ponziano
description: A Paleochristian catacomb at the second mile of the via Portuensis. The catacomb is noted for containing the original tombs of Pope Anastasius I and his son Pope Innocent I.

name: Catacomb of SS. Paul and Agatha
description: An important paleochristian complex of Malta, dating from the fourth to the ninth centuries AD.

name: Exokhi
description: Exokhi is the site of an early Rhodian necropolis on the island of Rhodes in the eastern Mediterranean.

name: Gemakhi
description: An ancient necropolis of uncertain date on the island of Rhodes.

name: Unnamed cemeteries
description: unnamed cemetery group (on Chalke)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 F3 unnamed cemetery (by Kamiros on Rhodos)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 G3 unnamed cemetery (by Rhodos)

name: Unnamed cemetery group
description: An unnamed ancient cemetery group included in the Barrington Atlas that is located near Ialysos on the island of Rhodos.

name: Belevi Mausoleum
description: A monumental Hellenistic tomb located at the modern Turkish village of Belevi (11km northeast of ancient Ephesus). The tomb may have been prepared for either Antigonus Monophthalmus or Lysimachus. The Seleucid king Antiochus II Theos may have been buried there subsequently.

name: Pontikokastro
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F4 Pontikokastro

name: Tomba di Fadilla
description: A Roman tomb on the Via Flaminia at Grottarossa that is associated with Annia Aurelia Fadilla, the daughter of the emperor Marcus Aurelius.

name: Tomb of P. Numisius Ligus
description: A large ara type tomb located outside the Porta di Boiano.

name: Sopka-2
description: A burial site on the banks of the Om River in western Siberia containing a number of non-contemporaneous Bronze Age tombs.

name: Chatby Necropolis
description: Understood to be the oldest necropolis in Alexandria that dates back to the Ptolemaic period and is located in the modern Chatby area of Alexandria.

name: Catacombs of Kom el Shoqafa
description: The Catacombs of Kom el Shoqafa contain Roman period tombs, second to fourth centuries A.D.

name: Gaimanova Mogila
description: A Scythian burial site containing burials from the fourth to second centuries B.C.; located near the Dnieper River, southeast of the modern village of Balky.

props.ancientName: Gaĭmanova Mogila, Gajmanova Mogila, Gaimanova Mogila

name: Dolmen de Kerhenry
description: A Neolithic tomb.

name: Necropolis of Pantalica
description: A large, rock-cut necropolis of over 5,000 tombs dating from the thirteenth to the seventh centuries BC.

name: Pontic rock-cut tombs
description: A number of monumental, rupestral tombs at ancient Amaseia (modern Amasya in Turkey) among which 5 large ones are mentioned by Strabo and attributed to the kings. Mount Harşena and the Rock-tombs of the Pontic Kings were submitted to the Tentative World Heritage Lists in 2005 by the Permanent Delegation of Turkey to UNESCO.

name: San Manno hypogeum
description: An Etruscan hypogeum tomb dating either to the third or the second century BCE. The tomb is located beneath the medieval church of San Manno in the comune of Perugia.

name: Mandzhikiny Burial Ground
description: Burial ground in Kalmykia, Russia with 17 burial mounds from the Bronze Age and Early Iron Age.

name: Castellaccio Europarco
description: A villa and a number of burials located along the Via Laurentina, dating from the fourth century BC to the second century AD.

name: Prato Rosello necropolis
description: An Etruscan necropolis with a number of tumulus-type tombs dating between the eighth and sixth centuries BC.

name: Etruscan necropolis of Dometaia
description: An Etruscan necropolis dating to the sixth century B.C.

name: Qila' el-Dabba
description: An Old Kingdom cemetery located in the Dakhla Oasis, Balat, Egypt, that contains the tombs of royal officials who governed the area of the oasis.

name: Treasury of Atreus
description: A Bronze Age tholos tomb with a corbeled dome and monumental ashlar facade on the Panagitsa Hill at Mycenae.

name: Dolmen of the four Maols
description: "The Dolmen of the four Maols" is a cist and dolmen tomb in County Mayo, Ireland.

name: Necropolis of Porta Vesuvio
description: A necropolis of Pompeii located outside the so-called "Porta Vesuvio".

name: Tomba dei Leoni Ruggenti
description: The so-called "Tomb of the Roaring Lions" at Veii is considered to be among the earliest, if not the earliest, known example of a painted tomb in Etruria. It is dated ca. 700-690 BCE.

name: South Necropolis (Pergamon)
description: An extensive Roman necropolis, with burials stretching back to at least the Hellenistic age, lies south and southwest of the so-called "Red Hall basilica" beneath the modern Turabey Mahallesi neighborhood of Bergama, Turkey.

name: Fag el-Gamous Cemetery
description: A large (300 acres), multiperiod (Old Kingdom through late Roman) cemetery located just beyond the arable land of the eastern Fayoum in Egypt. It likely served the nearby ancient settlements of Tanis, Philadelphia, and Selia.

name: Khirbet Kūfīn
description: Necropolis in the West Bank near the modern village of Beit Ummar dated to the Middle Bronze Age.

props.ancientName: Khirbet Kūfīn, Khirbat Kūfīn, Khirbet Koufin, Kusin

name: Pyramid of Senusret II
description: Senusret II was a pharaoh of the Twelfth dynasty of ancient Egypt.

name: Cemeteries around Caere
description: This place was created for Barrington Atlas map 43 (A2 no. 1) as a collective place representing ancient Etruscan necropoleis associated with the ancient city of Caere. While the Banditaccia necropolis is the largest and perhaps most well-known, this collective place should also include the Sorbo necropolis, the Monte Abatone necropolis, and the Greppe S. Angelo necropolis.

name: Villa delle Grotte at Grottarossa
description: A villa with beginnings in the early Republican period, continuing in use to the middle Imperial period.

name: Unnamed villas and a tomb near Pons Mulvius
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B2 no. 9 (villas (2) and tomb near Pons Mulvius)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C1 no. 18 (tomb near Nomentum)

name: Cemetery of Gordianus, Epimachus, Apronianus
description: A burial ground for the the Christian martyrs Gordianus, Epimachus, and Apronianus at the seventh mile of the Via Latina.

props.ancientName: Catacomb of Saints Gordian and Epimachus

name: Tombs near Villa Adriana
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 no. 30 (tombs near Villa Adriana)

name: So-Called Tomb of Clodius
description: The so-called Tomb of Clodius along the Via Appia near Castel Gandolfo.

name: Tomb of Priscilla
description: A Roman tomb along the Via Appia that belonged to one Priscilla, wife of Titus Flavius Abascanto, a freedman of the emperor Domitian.

name: Wadi Qitna
description: Late Roman-Early Byzantine cemetery at the point where the Wadi Qitna met the Nile. The graves were tumuli and covered 1200 meters. The site was deluged upon the creation of the High Aswan Dam.

name: Tomb of Cyrus the Great
description: The Tomb of Cyrus the Great is located about 1 km southwest of the palaces at Pasargadae. It is typically dated to ca. to 559-529 B.C.

name: Byzantine arcosolia
description: Byzantine tombs at the site belong to the sixth century when the Temple of Concordia was converted into a Christian church.

name: Catacombs of Generosa
description: A Paleochristian catacomb located at the sixth mile of the via Portuense.

name: Maleme
description: A Mycenaean tholos tomb located to the north of Margarites, Rethymno, Crete.

name: I Morroni
description: Morroni dei Marsi, the remains of two mausolea dating to the Roman period.

name: Buffa necropolis
description: One of the necropoleis of the ancient city of Selinous, on the Buffa hill in contrada Manuzza, to the NE of the ancient city

name: The Great Pyramid
description: The Great Pyramid of Khufu was built ca. 2580–2560 B.C. during the 4th dynasty of ancient Egypt. It initially stood to a height of 146.5 meters (481 feet).

name: Alyattes Tumulus
description: The tumulus of the Lydian king Alyattes.

name: Gyges Tumulus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F4 Gyges

name: Philopappos Monument
description: The Philopappos Monument, located on Mouseion Hill in Athens, is a funerary monument dedicated to Caius Julius Antiochus Epiphanes Philopappos, a prince of the Kingdom of Commagene, and dates after 116 CE.

name: Crypta Balbi
description: The Crypta Balbi, mentioned by name only in the Regionary Catalogues, most likely refers to a vaulted passageway built in the Campus Martius by the proconsul Lucius Cornelius Balbus in 15 BC, who also dedicated a theater there in the same year.

name: Pyramid of Senusret I
description: The Pyramid of Senusret I dates to the Twelfth Dynasty of ancient Egypt. It is located at el-Lisht near the pyramid of Amenemhat I, the father of Senusret I.

name: Roman Mausoleum of Villa Gordiani
description: The Roman Mausoleum of Villa Gordiani is a two-storey cylindrical tomb with pronaos executed in opus latericium. It fronts on the ancient via Praenestina. The rotunda has a diameter of more than 19 meters. Based on brick stamps, chronology is potentially wide and likely should be assigned anywhere in the period from Constantine I to Theodoric.

name: Tumba de Servilia (Servilia Tomb)
description: The Tumba de Servilia was constructed as a mausoleum for the Servilii family in the 1st century CE and is part of the Roman Necropolis of Carmo in Sevilla, Spain.

name: Documaci tumulus
description: An elite tomb structure constructed in the late 4th to early 3rd century BCE near the Hellenistic settlement of Callatis.

name: Catacombe di San Sebastiano
description: The catacombs of San Sebastiano located on the Via Appia are located near the church of the same name. These hypogeal tombs emerged in the third century CE and were linked initially to the relics of the apostles Peter and Paul.

name: Tombs of the Kings
description: The "Tombs of the Kings" archaeological site is a Hellenistic and Roman-period necropolis associated with the city of Nea Paphos and characterized by large, rock-cut, subterranean tomb structures.

name: Casal Bernocchi-Malafede
description: An archaeological area along the Via Ostiensis with evidence of human activity stretching from the Archaic period to the time of the Roman Empire. SIgnificant archaeological features include an extensive necropolis and a Roman bathing facility.

name: Meta Romuli
description: The so-called "Meta Romuli" was a pyramidal tomb located in the Ager Vaticanus in Rome. It likely dates to the first century BCE or the first century CE. Its extant superstructure was demolished in 1499. Remains of foundations and pavements that have been associated with the tomb were encountered in 1948-1949 during construction work in the first block of the north side of the present-day Via della Conciliazione.

name: Vapheio Tholos Tomb
description: The Vapheio Tholos Tomb is a Mycenaean tholos built around 1500 BC that is assumed to have held an elite burial or burials. It is best known for the two golden Minoan-style cups it housed.

name: Guglia di Marcello
description: The so-called "Guglia di Marcello" or "Aguglia d'Agosta" is a Roman funerary monument located along the ancient via Pompei that once connected Syracuse and Messina. It dates to the period from the first century B.C. to the first century A.D.

name: Mausoleum of Romulus
description: Tomb of Valerius Romulus (ca. AD 292/295 – 309), son of Maxentius, along the Via Appia.

name: Giants' grave of Sa Dom'è s'Orcu (Quartucciu)
description: A "Giant's tomb" of the fourteenth and thirteenth centuries B.C.

name: Tomb of Petosiris at Tuna el-Gebel
description: The Tomb of Petosiris at Tuna el-Gebel dates to the late fourth century BCE. Petosiris, also called Ankhefenkhons, served as the high priest of Thoth at Hermopolis during the 28th Dynasty of ancient Egypt.

props.ancientName: Tomb of Petosiris at Tuna el-Gebel

name: Tomb of Endymion (Olympia)
description: A lost monument to Endymion seen by Pausanius near the starting area of the stadium of Olympia.

name: Tomba delle Anatre
description: The Tomb of the Ducks at Veii is an early example of a painted Etruscan tomb. It dates to the second quarter of the seventh century BCE and was discovered in 1958 in the Riserva del Bagno necropolis.

name: Tomba Campana
description: The Grotta Campana at Veii is an Etruscan burial complex discovered by Giampietro Campana in 1843.

name: Tumulo di Montecalvario
description: An Etruscan tomb located at Castellina in Chianti in Italy.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 B3 unnamed unlabeled ((1 monument, 1 cemetery))

description: BAtlas mapped a large number of unnamed sites in map 34 gridsquare E2. The corresponding atlas directory entry cites AAA 27.28, 38, 39 without comment.
