name: Punta Moral Roman mausoleum
description: A Roman mausoleum dating to the early fourth century CE located at Punta del Moral, Isla de Canela, Ayamonte.

props.ancientName: Mausoleo romano de Punta del Moral

name: Tomb of Epaminondas
description: A burial mound described by Pausanias as the Tomb of Theban general Epaminondas. Located near Mantineia, east of Milia.

name: Cascina Torretta
description: Approximately 41 burials from a Roman cremation necropolis were excavated in 2021 at Cascina Torretta, a locality in the municipality of Calvignasco (southwest of Milan, Italy). The tombs were poorly preserved, appeared to be relatively shallow pit graves, and were likely connected to a rural settlement.

name: Cima tumulus
description: A rock-cut Etruscan tomb dating to the second half of the seventh century B.C.

name: Tumulo del tesoro
description: An Etruscan tumulus of the seventh century B.C.

name: Trayamar necropolis
description: Punic necropolis on the southern coast of the Iberian Peninsula dated to the 7th century BCE.

name: Pisciarelli necropolis
description: An Etruscan necropolis with tombs dated to the archaic period located near modern Civitavecchia in Italy.

name: Dhahr Mirzbaneh
description: A Bronze Age necropolis located near the village of 'Ayn Samiyah in the West Bank.

name: Tomba dell'Arciere
description: An Etruscan tumulus dating to the seventh century B.C. at the locality known as La Collina in the comune of San Casciano in Val di Pesa.

name: Pozzo Pantaleo
description: The 'Pozzo Pantaleo' is a Roman mausoleum that re-used a cistern of the early Imperial period. In a third phase, the site was repurposed as a shrine dedicated to the Christian Saint Pantaleon.

name: Necropoli Cappella
description: The Cappella necropolis preserves a complex of underground tombs arranged along the ancient road connecting Misenum and Cumae. The earliest tombs date to the late first century BCE.

name: Necropoli del Castellaccio
description: A necropolis at Falerii Veteres.

name: Post-Archaic necropolis at Poggio dei Cavallari
description: A necropolis located along a road that once led to the Mater Matuta sanctuary composed of approximately 50 tombs.

name: Belverde Necropoli della Cavetta
description: The Cavetta necropolis at Pantalica includes tombs and rock-cut houses of the prehistoric and later periods.

name: Kral Kaya Mezarları
description: Elite Hellenistic rock-cut tombs of the Pontic kingdom.

name: Gol Mod-2
description: Cemetery site in Arkhangai Province, Mongolia containing 98 terrace tombs associated with the Xiongnu culture.

name: Theban Tomb 359
description: The New Kingdom tomb of the ancient Egyptian workman Inherkhau is located at Deir el-Medina and is part of the Theban Necropolis.

name: Grave Circle A at Mycenae
description: Grave Circle A encloses a royal cemetery within the walls of the citadel of Mycenae; inside it are six shaft graves constructed around 1600 BC.

name: Pimentel, Necropoli di S'Acqua Salida e di Corongiu
description: A necropolis of the second millennium B.C.

name: Mausoleum of Lucius Pomponius Grecinus
description: A Roman funerary monument of the first century B.C. that is traditionally associated with the prefect Lucius Pomponius Grecinus.

name: Plemmyrion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 G4 Plemmyrion

name: Rocca Stefano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 D4 Rocca Stefano

name: Rotunda of Galerius
description: A brick rotunda of the fourth century AD ordered by the emperor Galerius for use as either a tomb or temple; later incorporated into the Christian Church of Agios Georgios. Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1988

name: Mausoleum of Bibi Maryam at Qalhat
description: The Mausoleum of Bibi Maryam at Qalhat was built for Baha al-Din Ayaz, the successor of Mahmud b. Ahmad al-Qusi al-Qalhati, the founder of the Hormuz empire which flourished from the eighth to the fourteenth century.

name: Buston VI Necropolis
description: Necropolis in Southern Uzbekistan with late Bronze Age remains.

name: Ipogeo dell'Oplita
description: A hypogeum tomb of the fourth century BCE located at Canusium.

name: Megali Magoula
description: A Middle to Late Bronze Age settlement located on the hill of Megali Magoula that includes Mycenaean chamber tombs.

name: Krasnyĭ mai͡ak
description: Late Scythian burial site on the Lower Dnieper River containing burials dated to the 3rd century BCE-2nd century CE.

name: Stepushka
description: Burial site in the Altai region dated to the Syanbi-Zhouzhan period (4th-5th century CE).

name: Hexagonal mausoleum
description: A hexagonal tomb, perhaps that of a Roman centurion, dating to the late second or early third century AD.

name: Casale di Giovio
description: The Casale di Giovio was originally a Roman funereal monument that was re-worked in seventeenth century to assume its current form.

name: Pyramid of Djedefre
description: The Pyramid of Djedefre, a Fourth Dynasty pharaoh, at Abu Rawash.

name: Qaziqulu necropolis
description: Burial ground near the village of Qaziqulu in Azerbaijan's Tovuz district. The site contains a number of burials dated from the 10th-7th centuries BCE. The site was inundated during the construction of the Tovuzchay reservoir in 2016.

name: Torre del Paladino
description: A Roman tower-type mausoleum located near Mondragone, Italy.

name: Sese Grande
description: The "Sese Grande" or "Sese del Re" is a prehistoric tomb on the island of Pantelleria. Sesi are megalithic tomb structures of the second millennium B.C. that are connected with the Sesiote culture.

name: Mogil'nik Ashpyl
description: Burial site in the Minusinsk Basin (southern Siberia) containing over 50 burials dated to the 1st millennium BCE.

name: Necropolis at Archontiko
description: A cemetery used by Macedonians in the area of Pella from the Early Iron Age to the Early Hellenistic Period.

name: Quartucciu
description: The archaeological site of Pill'è Matta preserves remains of a Punic-Roman necropolis in use from the fourth century B.C. until the fifth century A.D.

name: Sepolcro di via Bisignano
description: An ancient Roman brick tomb of the second century that is located in the Via Bisignano between the fifth mile of Via Appia Antica and the seventh kilometer of Via Appia Nuova. The tomb has also been known as the Temple of Salus. Its typology is comparable to that of the tomb of Publius Aelius Callistion.

name: Akbou mausoleum
description: An ancient mausoleum located at modern Akbou on the north slope of a prominent hill in the southwestern neighborhood of Bouzeroual (Béjaïa Province, Algeria).

name: funeral monuments of the Via Iulia Augusta
description: A Roman necropolis located along the course of the Via Iulia Augusta in modern Albenga, Italy. The archaeological area includes the remains of at least nine tombs dating to the first and second centuries CE. These family tombs faced the ancient roadway. Nino Lamboglia carried out the first archaeological fieldwork here in the 1930s.

props.ancientName: funeral monuments of the via Giulia Augusta

name: Pyramid-complex of Senusret III
description: Funerary complex of King Senusret III at Dahshur including a pyramid.

name: Martini-Marescotti Tomb
description: The so-called Martini-Marescotti Tomb is an Etruscan tomb located in the Monte Abatone necropolis of ancient Caere. The tomb was built in the late sixth or early fifth century BCE and is composed of a dromos and three funereal chambers covered by a tumulus. The tomb contained a substantial funereal deposit that included painted Attic wares.

name: Tanella di Pitagora
description: A third to second century B.C. tomb in the hinterland of Cortona.

name: Tomb of the Nasonii
description: The tomb of the Nasonii family is an ancient Roman hypogeum-type tomb along the Via Flaminia in Grottarossa in the northern outskirts of Rome. The tomb was exposed during road works in 1674.

name: Funerary chapel of Shepenupet I
description: Funerary chapel of the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Shepenupet I, daughter of King Osorkon III.

name: Abu Rawash
description: Pharaonic archaeological site located 8 km north of Giza, including the pyramid of King Djedefre and several necropolises.

name: Bratolyubovskii Kurgan
description: A Scythian burial mound dated to the fourth century B.C. located near the village of Bratolyubivka in Kherson Oblast, Ukraine.

props.ancientName: Bratolyubovskii Kurgan, Bratoli͡ubovskiĭ Kurgan, Bratoljubovka Kurgan, Bratolyubivka Kurgan, Bratolyubovka Kurgan

name: Carceri Vecchie
description: The so-called "le Carceri Vecchie" is a first-century CE Roman mausoleum. Its original position on the Via Appia placed it outside the walls of ancient Capua.

name: Başur Höyük
description: A Bronze Age funereal mound located in Turkey's south-eastern Siirt Province.

name: Tomb of Publius Aelius Callistion
description: The tomb of Publius Aelius Callistion, a freedman (libertus) of the emperor Hadrian, is colloquially known as the "Sedia del Diavolo". It is located in a piazza that derives its name from the tomb's occupant (piazza Elio Callistio) in Rome's Trieste neighborhood.

name: Tre Ponti necropolis
description: A necropolis along the Via Amerina, likely in use from the third century BC until the third century AD.

name: Catacombs of Domitilla
description: A Christian catacomb located on the via Ardeatina.

name: Satricum, Southwest Necropolis
description: The Southwest Necropolis located at Satricum.

name: Tomb of Themistocles
description: The so-called Tomb of Themistocles at Drapetsona.

name: S’Ena ’e Thomes
description: A well-preserved 'giant's tomb' dating ca. 2000-1600 BC.

name: Sphoungaras
description: A hill located several hundred meters north of the Minoan center of Gournia, where a series of Minoan tombs were excavated beginning in the early twentieth century.

name: Amphissa Tholos
description: A Mycenaean tholos tomb located southeast of Amphissa, Phokida.

name: Tomb of the Kings near Enkomi
description: Royal tombs of the Middle Bronze Age located near Enkomi.

name: Potto Codinu necropolis
description: A necropolis in use from the Neolithic period through to the Iron Age with numerous hypogeum-type tombs.

name: Tomb of Tiberius Claudius Secundus
description: A Roman tomb along the Via Appia.

name: Valley of the Kings
description: A valley in Egypt where Pharaonic burials took place from the sixteenth to the eleventh centuries B.C. (Eighteenth to the Twentieth Dynasties).

name: Mausoleo di Ummidia Quadratilla
description: A Roman tomb located in the archaeological area of Casinum that dates to either the first or second centuries CE.

name: Mausoleum of Lucius Sempronius Atratinus
description: A first-century B.C. circular funerary monument at Gaeta.

name: Vallerano
description: A necropolis located on the southern outskirts of Rome. The necropolis is located near a villa site along a road running through the suburbium of Rome. Excavated between 1992 and 1993.

name: Grotte Scalina
description: A monumental rock-cut Etruscan tomb that dates to the later fourth century B.C.

name: Pyramid of Pepi I at Saqqarah
description: One of the pyramids at Saqqarah in Egypt. Pepi I was a pharaoh of the Sixth Dynasty and reigned from ca. 2332–2287 B.C.

name: Funerary chapel of Ankhnesneferibre
description: Presumed remains of the funerary chapel of the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Ankhnesneferibre.

name: Catacomb of Balbina
description: The Catacomb of Balbina in the suburbium of Rome lies between the via Appia antica and the via Ardeatina in Rome's Ardeatino district. This catacomb is part of the larger Callixtian complex that includes the catacombs of Callixtus and Santi Marco e Marcelliano. The Depositio episcoporum mentions the catacomb, indicating that Pope Mark was buried "in Balbinae" in 336.

name: Hypogeum of Clepsina
description: A late eighth to early seventh century B.C. hypogeum tomb on the main plateau of ancient Caere.

name: Karacamirli Tepe 5
description: Necropolis in western Azerbaijan containing 46 burials dated to the Late Bronze Age/early Iron Age, the Late Antique period, and the 14th century CE.

name: Columbarium in via Luigi Tosti
description: Excavations in 2022 revealed the remains of a Roman tomb of small dimensions, likely of the columbarium type. The tomb included fragments of a sarcophagus in white marble and an inscribed funerary altar of the second century CE. The altar commemorates the life of one Valeria Laeta, daughter of Publius, who lived 13 years and 7 months. The absence of traditional pre-Christian epigraphic formulae led ministry archaeologists to speculate that the tomb is of a Christian nature.

name: Tumulus MM
description: A funeral mound of the latter eighth century BC, possibly associated with the Phrygian king Gordias.

name: Lisht
description: Lisht or el-Lisht, located on the west bank of the Nile River south of Cairo, is the site of a Middle Kingdom necropolis that included both royal and ranking elite burials. Two pyramid complexes were built at the site: one for Amenemhat I and the other for Senusret I.

name: Chûn Quoit
description: Chûn Quoit is a dolmen-type tomb located in Cornwall. The quoit was likely originally covered by a barrow.

name: Belas Knap Longbarrow
description: Belas Knap is a Neolithic chamber tomb located on Cleeve Hill in Gloucestershire, England. The tomb is dated ca. 3800 BC.

name: Tomb of Senwosret III
description: The tomb of Senwosret III, a pharaoh of the 12th Dynasty in Egypt.

name: Necropolis of Oretum
description: An archaeological site connected with ancient Oretum, now located adjacent to the Ermita-Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Oreto y Zuqueca.

name: Necropolis at Sutrium
description: Numerous rock-cut tombs are evident at Sutri, most dating from the Hellenistic period and later. In archaeological terms the tombs are poorly understood.

name: Theban Tomb 414 (TT 414)
description: Originally the Saite tomb of Ankh-Hor, this site was reused from the 4th century BCE by the Late Dynastic/Ptolemaic period family of Pa-di-Amun-neb-nesut-tawy, a high-ranking priest of Khonsu.

name: Abaton
description: An ancient site designated as the burial place of Osiris-Serapis.

name: Nag el-Hasaya
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B4 Nag el-Hasaya

name: Bartlow Hills
description: Site of a Roman barrow cemetery

name: Lamyatt
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Lamyatt

name: Per-Ptah
description: The site of Per-Ptah, meaning "House of Ptah", lies some 90 km south of Aswan and received a temple dedicated to pharaoh Ramesses II. Some of the architectural and sculptural remains of the temple were dismantled and relocated to the site of New Kalabsha in the 1960s during the construction of the Aswan High Dam.

name: Roman Tomb at Mersea
description: A Roman barrow tomb on Mersea Island.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 84 E1 unnamed tumulus (near Tanais (3 clusters))

name: Awlad Azzaz Site
description: Late 18th Dynasty tomb site near the towns of Nag ed Deir and Awlad Azzaz in Egypt.

name: Tomb of the Winged Demons
description: The so-called Tomb of the Winged Demons (Sovana, Italy), a third-century BC Etruscan tomb located in the Poggio Felceto necropolis.

name: Dendra
description: Bronze Age cemetery near the Mycenean settlement of Midea.

name: Tomb of Seuthes III
description: An elaborate tomb located near Kazanlak, Bulgaria. The Tomb of Seuthes III, who was King of the Odrysian Kingdom from ca. 331 to ca. 300 BC, is found in the Valley of the Thracian Rulers.

name: Madinat Hamad 2 Burial Mound Field (Karzakkan)
description: A necropolis of the early Dilmun culture, dating to the second millennium B.C.

name: Mogil'nik Maĭtan
description: Bronze Age burial ground in east-central Kazakhstan.

name: Tartas-1
description: Burial site near the banks of the Tartas River in Western Siberia containing over 400 burials, mostly associated with the Krotovo (Chernoozero) and Andronovo (Fedorovo) cultures and dated to the 3rd-2nd millennia BCE

name: Grave of Charmylos
description: The Grave of Charmylos is a third-century BCE vaulted tomb located on Kos. The site is understood as a heroön dedicated to the legendary king Charmylos. The ancient site was dismantled and partially reused in the church of Tímios Stávros.

name: Solenyĭ Dol
description: Burial complex in the southern Ural Mountains region containing a number of Late Antique burials.

name: Royal Tombs of Vergina
description: The Macedonian royal necropolis at Vergina.

name: Heroön
description: The so-called Heroön at Sagalassos dominates the Upper Agora in the northwest sector of the city.

name: Giant's tomb at Lu Brandali
description: A passage grave associated with the nuragic complex at Lu Brandali near Santa Teresa Gallura.

name: Bolʹshoĭ Salbykskiĭ kurgan
description: The Great Salbyk Mound is the largest burial mound in the Khakassia region of Russia. The mastaba-like tomb, associated with the Bronze Age Tagar culture, was constructed in the 7th century BCE, and may have had an astronomical function.

name: Gallardet Dolmen
description: A large dolmen located near Le Pouget in Languedoc, France.

name: Tomb of the sons of Sextus Pompeius Justus
description: A tomb on the Via Appia dated ca. 50 CE.

name: Tomb of Septimia Galla
description: A Roman tomb at the sixth mile of the Via Appia.

name: Qarn al-Harf
description: Bronze age burial site associated with Wadi Suq culture. The site is located alongside a limestone ridge in the Sir Plain east of the city of Ras al-Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates.

name: Mausoleo di San Vito at Puteoli
description: A mausoleum in the so-called San Vito necropolis that dates to the first century A.D.

name: Mausoleum of Lucius Munatius Plancus
description: A Roman mausoleum that is associated with Lucius Munatius Plancus.

name: Tomb of Tutankhamun (KV62)
description: The rock-cut tomb of the Egyptian pharaoh Tutankhamun (r. ca. 1332-1323 BC), discovered by Howard Carter in 1922.

name: Cross dyke and bowl barrow on the northern spur of Beacon Hill
description: A cross dyke in Burghclere, Basingstoke and Deane, England, United Kingdom.

name: Roman Mausoleum
description: A Roman tower-type tomb that some local traditions label as the tomb of Cnaeus Pompeius Magnus. The structure is located at the so-called Villa di Pompeo, south of Castel Gandolfo in Italy.

name: Loughcrew (Megalithic Landscape)
description: Loughcrew (County Meath, Ireland) is the site of a funereal landscape of the fourth millennium BCE that includes more than twenty megalithic tombs spread across four hilltop areas: Carnbane East, Carnbane West, Carrickbrack, and Patrickstown Hill.

name: Psaropyrgos
description: An ancient temple of Poseidon, known today as the "Tomb of Homer", located on a height at the northern end of the Aegean island of Ios. Some funerary epigraphy has also been noted at the site.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 unnamed cemetery (on Rheneia)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 unnamed cemetery (near Oikonomos, on Paros)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A5 unnamed cemetery (on Therasia)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 unnamed cemetery (NW Kos)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 unnamed cemetery (cluster W Kos)

name: Unnamed cemetery
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 unnamed cemetery (on Nisyros)

name: Tatarlı
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 E5 Tatarlı

name: Necropoli del Cavone di Monte Li Santi
description: A monumental, rock-cut Faliscan necropolis of Narce in use from ca. 750 BCE until the fourth century BCE.

name: Odigitria tholos cemetery
description: A Bronze Age necropolis containing at least two tholos-type tombs located to the north of the Odigitria monastery, Pigaidakia, Iraklio, Crete.

name: Tumulus of Glimes
description: A Gallo-Roman tumulus located near Incourt, Belgium.

name: Trou de Billemont Tumulus
description: The Trou de Billemont Tumulus is a second century AD Gallo-Roman tumulus in Belgium.

name: Tumulus aux Six Frères
description: The Tumulus aux Six Frères (tumulus of the six brothers) is a Gallo-Roman burial mound at Dhuy in Belgium.

name: Twee Tommen
description: A twin Gallo-Roman burial mound located to the east of Monte Aachen, Belgium.

name: Al-Khazneh
description: An elaborate, rock-cut monument at Petra built ca. 100 BC to AD 200 known as "Al-Khazneh" or "The Treasury". The Nabatean structure, originally used as a tomb, dates to the reign of Aretas IV Philopatris.

name: Necropolis of Mesu ‘e Montes
description: The Necropolis of Mesu ‘e Montes contains at least 18 tombs of the domus de janas type. The necropolis dates from the later Neolithic period to the middle Bronze Age.

name: Necropolis of Su Crucifissu Mannu
description: A prehistoric necropolis at Porto Torres, Sardinia, containing at least 22 domus de ianas type tombs.

name: Jebel Moya
description: Jebel Moya is an archaeological site located in the southern part of the Gezira Plain in Sudan. The 25-acre site was the locus of the largest-known pastoralist necropolis in Africa, with more than 3,000 burials dating between 5000 BCE and 500 CE. Sir Henry Wellcome first excavated the site from 1911 to 1914.

name: La Hougue Bie
description: A Neolithic ritual site that includes a passage grave dating ca. 4000-3500 BCE.

name: Anghelu Ruju necropolis
description: A pre-nuragic necropolis discovered in 1903.

name: Tumulus of Noirmont
description: A second-century Gallo-Roman tumulus at Chastre, Belgium.

name: Tumulus of Xhendremael
description: The Tumulus of Xhendremael (known also as Li Tombe di Hên'mâl) is a Gallo-Roman burial mound near Xhendremael in Belgium. In 1933 the remains of a Roman villa were discovered in its vicinity.

name: Tumulus of Avennes
description: The Tumulus of Avennes is a Gallo-Roman burial mound dated to the Flavian period located in Liège Braives, Belgium.

name: Tumulus of Avernas
description: A Gallo-Roman burial mound located near Avernas-le-Bauduin, Belgium.

name: Los Villares necropolis
description: The site of a pre-Roman Iberian necropolis, dating sixth to fourth centuries BCE located in the municipality of Hoya-Gonzalo (Albacete, Spain).

name: Pokrovka-4 mound
description: Burial mound near the Sentelek River in Altay, Russia, containing remains associated with the Afanasievo Culture (31st-29th centuries BCE) and the Pazyryk Culture (4th-3rd centuries BCE).

name: Yamaan Us-I
description: Burial site in Zavkhan Province, Mongolia containing over 150 burials dated to the Xiongnu period.

name: Kimonian monuments
description: A rock-cut double tomb on the northwestern slope of the Hill of the Muses in Athens, traditionally associated with Kimon. A 3rd-century AD inscription identifies the owner at that time as Zosimianos, indicating re-use of the structure.

name: Early Christian Necropolis of Pécs (Sopianae)
description: A series of decorated early Christian tombs dating to the fourth century CE is located in the town of Sopianae, in the Roman Province of Pannonia. The Early Christian Necropolis of Pécs was enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000.

name: Tusson tumuli
description: An assemblage of four Middle Neolithic tumuli located in the territory of the commune of Tusson in the French department of Charente. The four tumuli are known colloquially as "tumulus de la Justice", "le Petit Dognon", "le Gros Dognon", and "le Vieux Breuil".

name: Dolmen de la Pierre Blanche
description: The ruinous remains of a dolmen-type tomb, its tumulus no longer extant, are located on the same axis as the Tusson tumuli.

name: Tomb of Virgil
description: The so-called tomb of the Latin poet Virgil.

name: Tomb of Nigidius Alleius Maius
description: The Tomb of Nigidius Alleius Maius was excavated in 2017. The tomb includes a 'res gestae'-style inscription in Latin that describes the life, career, and patronage of the decedent. Although the name of the decedent is no longer extant, the tomb has been linked to prominent citizen Nigidius Alleius Maius.

name: Pottenbrunn burial ground
description: Burial site near modern Pottenbrunn, Austria, containing 43 burials dated to the Roman period.

name: Silwan necropolis
description: Iron Age cemetery located on the eastern slope of the Kidron Valley, containing burials dated from the 9th-7th centuries BCE.

name: Naga ed-Deir
description: The necropolis associated with the unlocated city This (Thinis), capital city of the unified Egyptian kingdom in the Early Dynastic period. The necropolis stretches for 1.5 kilometers and contains thousands of burials from the Predynastic period through the early Coptic period, with particularly large numbers of burials from the Predynastic period (ca. 3800–3200 BCE) and the late Old Kingdom-First Intermediate Period (ca. 2300–2061 BCE).

props.ancientName: Naga ed-Deir, Naga ed-Dêr, Naj al Dayr, Naj‘ ad Dayr, Nag’ el Deir

name: Ananauri Big Kurgan No. 3
description: Bronze Age burial site of the Alazani-Bedeni culture near the Alazani River in eastern Georgia. The site is dated to the first half of the 24th century BCE.

name: Gasr Doga
description: The mausoleum of Gasr Doga is a monumental, elite Libico-Punic tomb built in the Tarhuna area.

name: Wadi Scetaf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 G3 Wadi Scetaf

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 G3 unnamed monument (el-Amud mausolea near Wadi N'f'd)

name: Eightercua
description: A megalithic tomb dated ca. 1700 BC located near Waterville, County Kerry, Ireland.

name: Gournes Cemetery
description: Early Minoan cemetery located in Gournes, Crete, containing 37 tombs. The site was largely destroyed by the installation of irrigation pipes and the building of the Hellenic Centre for Marine Research and its associated parking lot.

name: Komninon (Macedonian tomb), Stavroupolis
description: Stavroupolis is the site of the largest Macedonian tomb known in Thrace.

name: Funerary Chamber of Manlia Felicitas
description: A subterranean funerary chamber with three frescoes walls depicting animal and vegetal life, located around the third mile of the Via Appia.

name: Monte Tamburino necropolis
description: A necropolis with inhumations dating to the fifth and fourth centuries B.C.

name: Diavolino II tomb
description: An Etruscan tomb of the seventh century B.C.

name: Bliznet͡sy
description: Iron age burial mound near Dnipro (Dnipropetrovsk).

name: Karban-I
description: Burial site in the Northern Altai region containing remains associated with the Bulan-Koby culture (2nd-5th centuries CE).

name: Mausoleum Campo Barbarico
description: The Campo Barbarico Mausoleum is a third-century CE Roman "temple tomb" located at the fourth mile of the Via Latina. The tomb's position is immediately to the south of the archaeological area of the Tombs of the Via Latina. In 2022, additional in situ tracts of the Via Latina's pavement were excavated adjacent to the mausoleum.

name: Royal Hypogeum at Qatna
description: Tomb VI at Qatna, known as the Royal Hypogeum, is a tomb complex located approximately 12 meters below the Royal Palace of Qatna. The tomb consists of a long corridor and 4 burial chambers, and contained approximately 20 burials.

name: Pyramids of Queens
description: There are three satellite pyramids, typically referred to as "Queen's pyramids", associated with the pyramid of Menkaure on the Giza plateau.

name: Western necropolis at Egnatia/Gnathia
description: The western necropolis at Egnatia/Gnathia includes some tombs of the Messapian culture dating from the middle of the fourth to the second centuries B.C.

name: Pietrera tomb
description: An Etruscan tholos tomb of the seventh century B.C. at Vetulonia.

name: Belvedere tomb
description: An Etruscan tomb of the seventh century B.C.

name: Tomb of the Shields and Chairs, Caere
description: The so-called Tomb of the Shields and Chairs at Caere dates ca. 520 - 500 B.C. The organization of this tomb is similar to that of the Tomb of the Capitals and the Tomb of the Cornice. It has a short dromos and a large vestibule. In the vestibule are two seats carved from the tuff bedrock as well as a series of hanging shields carved in relief.

name: Tomb of the Five Chairs, Caere
description: An Etruscan tomb at Caere dated ca. 625-600 B.C.

name: Alto do Colaride
description: A Roman necropolis located in a natural cave.

name: Belevi Tumulus
description: The Belevi tumulus may date to the early Hellenistic period.

name: Tuvixeddu Punic necropolis
description: A Punic necropolis near Caralis in Sardinia.

name: Necropolis of Filigosa
description: A Chalcolithic necropolis near Macomer in Sardinia.

name: Heroon of Eretria
description: A triangular enclosure with approximately 19 burials (both cremation and inhumation) dating to the Geometric period (ca. 750 BC - ca. 630 BC).

name: Pozzuolo necropolis
description: A necropolis of Etruscan Veii.

name: Malthi chamber tomb
description: A Mycenaean chamber tomb at Malthi, located near the Greek village of Vasiliko.

name: Strozzacapponi necropolis
description: The Strozzacapponi necropolis is an assemblage of Etruscan tombs dating from the third to the first centuries BCE. The chamber tomb is the predominant typology. The necropolis is located near Perugia in Umbria.

name: Sepolcreto Ostiense
description: The modern toponym "Sepolcreto Ostiense" refers to a portion of what was once a vast ancient necropolis in Rome, located between the Rupe di San Paolo and the bend of the Tiber River. This necropolis was purportedly the place where the apostle Paul was buried and, subsequently, the Basilica of St. Paul Outside the Walls developed nearby. The archaeological evidence in the necropolis provides insight into the changing funeral customs in the second and third centuries CE, when the predominant funeral custom was gradually shifting from cremation to inhumation.

name: Tomb of Gaius Vestorius Priscus
description: The Tomb of Gaius Vestorius Priscus is located in Pompeii's Porta Vesuvio necropolis. Gaius Vestorius Priscus was an aedile and was granted 2,000 sesterces by the town council for his tomb and funeral. The tomb is decorated with paintings depicting a funereal meal.

name: Tomb of Arellia Tertulla
description: The Tomb of Arellia Tertulla, daughter of Numerius and wife of Veius Fronto.

name: Theban necropolis
description: Collective name for the complex of tombs and temples on the western bank of the Nile, opposite the city of Thebes/Luxor. The necropolis was used for royal and elite burials throughout the Pharaonic period.

name: Sepolcro dei Pancrazi
description: Sepolcro dei Pancrazi is a Roman tomb on the Via Latina dated to the late first or early second century CE. It belonged to a collegium funerarium (funerary college) of the Pancratii, a name attested in two extant inscriptions. The burial chambers are well-appointed with floor mosaics and frescoed walls, as well as stucco decorations.

name: Mineralovodsky burial ground
description: Burial site in the Northern Caucasus containing burials dated from the 6th-4th centuries BCE.

name: Sheikh Abd el-Qurna
description: A necropolis located on the West Bank at Thebes in Upper Egypt that is now part of the archaeological area of Deir el-Bahari.

name: Tomb of Lucius Sepunius Sandilianus
description: The Tomb of Lucius Sepunius Sandilianus is a Roman-period tomb, possibly of tholos typology, located in the Porta Nocera necropolis of Pompeii.

name: Lefkandi Heroon(?)
description: Rescue excavations in 1980 documented the remains of an unusual funerary structure dated ca. 1000 to 950 BCE that contained a double human burial alongside a horse burial. The structure's scale (50 by 13.8 m) and nature have led some scholars to identify it as a heroon.

name: Mausoleum of Helena
description: An ancient tomb located on the ancient Via Labicana (now along the Via Casilina) that was built by Constantine I and used as the tomb for his mother, Helena.

name: Necropolis of Campovalano
description: A Picene necropolis located near Campovalano (Campli) in the province of Teramo. While more than 600 tombs were identified, there may have been as many as 20,000 originally.

name: Hypogeum of Trebius Giustus
description: A tomb of the fourth century A.D. located on the Via Latina.

name: Mastaba of Ptahshepses at Abusir
description: The mastaba-style tomb of Ptahshepses, vizier to the Fifth Dynasty Pharaoh Nyuserre Ini. Following the intial elite burials in the Old Kingdom, the mastaba became a funerary site for new burials during the Late Period.

name: Mycenaean cemetery at Achaia Clauss
description: A Mycenaean-era cemetery located near the modern-day Achaia Clauss vineyard southeast of Patras, Greece.

name: Pyramid of Khafre
description: The Pyramid of Khafre is the second-largest of the ancient Egyptian Pyramids at Giza and served as the tomb of the pharaoh Khafre (Chefren), of the Fourth Dynasty of ancient Egypt.

name: Shore Temple of Mahabalipuram
description: The so-called "Shore Temple" of Mahabalipuram is a granite-built tomb at the "Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram" UNESCO World Heritage Site. It dates to the eighth century CE.

name: Gol Mod-1
description: Cemetery site in Arkhangai Province, Mongolia containing 483 tombs associated with the Xiongnu culture.

name: Tomba di Mezzavia
description: A single-chamber tomb carved in tufo bedrock at Cortona dates from the third and second centuries BCE. The Tomba di Mezzavia was discovered in 1950.

name: Pyramid of Userkaf
description: A Fifth Dynasty pyramid at Saqqarah.

name: Ipogeo dei Cristallini
description: A hypogeum tomb of the Hellenistic period in Neapolis (now Naples, Italy).

name: Umm el-Qa'ab
description: A necropolis at Abydos, Egypt, with royal tombs from the Pre-dynastic period as well as from the Early Dynastic (First and Second dynasties) period.

name: Burgast
description: Area in the Altai region of extreme western Mongolia containing a number of burials and other structures from the Bronze Age (Bulan-Kobin Culture), Turkic, and Medieval periods.

name: Mangadu Megalithic Site
description: An archaeological site with Iron Age funerary megaliths located in the vicinity of the modern Moonamkutty-Mangad Road in the northeastern portion of India's Kollam municipality. The site was damaged by cashewnut tree farming and is now not visible due to habitation on the site.

name: Djub-i Gauhar
description: 9th-7th centuries BCE Iron Age cemetery site near modern Eyvanki in Iran. The excavated site comprises 56 stone-lined tombs, 9 pit graves, and 1 pithos grave.

name: Mycenaean cemetery at Aidonia
description: Mycenaean burial ground located approximately 8 kilometers west of Nemea containing 20 tombs dated to the 15th century BCE. All but two of the burials were looted in the 1970s, but the illicit finds were retrieved and repatriated to Greece in 1996.

name: Barnwood Roman cemetery
description: Site of Romano-British inhumation and cremation cemetery.

name: Grotta dei Massacci
description: The so-called Grotta dei Massacci located in Osteria Nuova, municipality of Frasso Sabino, Italy, is a monumental, vaulted subterranean tomb structure that likely dates to the second century CE.

name: Columbarium of Caius Scribonius Menophilus
description: The Columbarium of Caius Scribonius Menophilus is located on the grounds of the Doria Pamphili in Rome. The well-preserved columbarium dates to the late republican and early imperial periods and is noted for its wall paintings.

name: Catacomb of the Nunziatella
description: The Catacomb of the Nunziatella or the Catacomb of the Annunziatella is an ancient catacomb located in Rome's Ardeatina neighborhood in the via di Grotta Perfetta. The catacomb was excavated on the site of a large Republican-period necropolis and does not seem to have been connected with any Christian martyrs.

name: Yüceören necropolis
description: Burial ground near Yüceören Hill in Cilicia containing tombs from the Hellenistic, Roman, and Byzantine periods.

name: Pyramidal mausoleum of Maktar
description: Representative location based on OpenStreetMap

name: Casetta Etruscan necropolis
description: The Necropolis della Casetta at Blera contains numerous dado-type tombs.

name: Peschiera necropolis
description: An Etruscan necropolis at Tuscania in use from the seventh until the first century B.C.

name: Mausoleo di Monte dell'Incastro
description: A circular, domed, apsidal subterranean structure of the Hadrianic period that is referred to as a tomb but may have had a cultic function.

name: Hypogeum of the Aurelii
description: A third-century AD hypogeum tomb on the Esquiline Hill in Rome with both pagan and Paleochristian iconography.

name: Ipogeo delle Ghirlande
description: The so-called Hypogeum of the Garlands is an ancient tomb located at the tenth mile of the ancient Via Latina. The tomb dates to either the first or second century CE.

name: Puntone necropolis
description: An Etruscan necropolis dating to the seventh-fifth centuries B.C. The ancient settlement associated with the site may have been Palmule.

name: Carrowmore Megalithic Cemetery
description: A grouping of fourth-millennium BC Neolithic monuments located on the Cúil Irra peninsula near Sligo, Ireland.

name: Duurlig Nars
description: Xiongnu tomb site near the modern village of Bayan-Adarga, Khentii, Mongolia. The site contains burials dated from the 4th century BCE to the 2nd century CE.

name: Phaleron cemetery (Attica)
description: A cemetery at Phaleron with burials ranging in date from the Archaic to the Classical period.

name: Sep. Bibuli
description: The tomb of C. Publicius Bibulus, a plebeian aedile, located at Rome at the foot of the Capitoline Hill. It most likely dates to the close of the Republican period.

name: Necropoli del Fusco
description: The Fusco necropolis is the largest and oldest burial area in Greek Syracuse. Located within contrada Canalicchio, the site was excavated by Paolo Orsi in 1893.

name: Tomb of Eumachia
description: A monumental exedra-type tomb built at the Porta Nocera between ca. A.D 1-30 that served as the tomb for the family of Eumachia.

name: Dra' Abu el-Naga'
description: A necropolis for pharaohs of the Seventeenth Dynasty of ancient Egypt.

name: Tophet de Salammbô
description: A hybrid sanctuary and necropolis sacred to Tanit and Baal.

name: Alyscamps
description: Alyscamps is a necropolis located outside the walls of Arles, France.

name: Grotta della Vipera
description: The so-called "Grotta della Vipera" is the hypogeum tomb of a Roman woman named Atilia Pomptilla. It dates to the first century CE.

name: Priddy Nine Barrows
description: The place known as Priddy Nine Barrows is a collection of round barrows, dating from the Bronze Age located near Priddy in Somerset, England.

name: Tomb of Aulus Umbricius Scaurus
description: A tomb at Pompeii's Herculaneum gate that may be that of Aulus Umbricius Scaurus.

name: Fatimid Cemetery in Aswan
description: The Fatimid Cemetery in Aswan includes tombs dating from the seventh to the twelfth centuries CE. The site is included in the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Nubian Monuments from Abu Simbel to Philae".

name: via Taranto columbaria
description: Two Roman columbaria are located under the present via Pescara, 2. This accounts for the tomb being referred to interchangeably with two modern, street-derived toponyms, namely "via Taranto" and "via Pescara". These tombs are situated near the ancient crossroads of the via Latina and the via Labicana.

name: Great Dolmen of Comenda da Igreja
description: A megalithic dolmen of the fourth to third millennia BC.

name: Minshat Abu Omar
description: Cemetery site in Northern Egypt with burials from the predynastic period as well as the late Roman period.

name: Sepolcro dei Veienti
description: The so-called "Sepolcro dei Veienti" is a ruined tomb of Roman imperial date. Built in opus caementicium and travertine, only the core of the rectangular structure remains. Likely located along the ancient Via Veientana, the tomb today stands in the modern Parco Papacci.

name: Castel Malnome (Ponte Galeria)
description: An imperial necropolis with at least 326 tombs was identified at modern Castel Malnome (Ponte Galeria) in Italy. Archaeological evidence marks these as burials belonging to working-class people of the Roman imperial period, with most graves having either no grave goods or very few. The osteoarchaeological analysis led excavators to speculate that the individuals buried here many have worked in the salt flats.

props.ancientName: necropoli imperiale di Castel Malnome (Ponte Galeria) (La)

name: Tert-Aba
description: Mound site in Khakassia containing burials associated with the Bronze Age Karasuk culture.

name: Sepulcretum
description: A modern appellation of the ancient Iron Age necropolis uncovered along the Sacra Via adjacent to the Temple of Antoninus and Faustina in April 1902. Giacomo Boni and others conducted extensive excavations at the site.

name: Tomb of Quintus Sulpicius Maximus
description: The tomb of the boy poet Quintus Sulpicius Maximus on the Via Salaria in Rome. He died in 94 CE, aged just 11 years.

name: Via della Monachina necropolis
description: A necropolis at the thirteenth kilometer of the Via Aurelia excavated between 2007 and 2011. A total of 332 tombs were identified at the site.

name: Uvecik Tepe
description: Uvecik Tepe or Üvecik Tepe is a tumulus in the Troad built for one Festus, a freedman of the emperor Caracalla.

name: Xylokeratidi cemetery
description: A Mycenaean necropolis located on the island of Amorgos that includes several chamber tombs.

name: Yığma Tepe Tumulus
description: The Yığma Tepe Tumulus is the largest ancient tumulus in the vicinity of Pergamon.

name: Maltepe (Pergamon)
description: The Maltepe tumulus at Pergamon is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Pergamon and its Multi-Layered Cultural Landscape".

name: Tumulus Tepe A, Pergamon
description: Tumulus Tepe A at Pergamon is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Pergamon and its Multi-Layered Cultural Landscape".

name: Libyco-Punic Mausoleum of Dougga
description: A royal mausoleum, dating to the second century BCE, located at ancient Thugga (modern Dougga in Tunisia).

name: Necropolis of San Germano
description: Burial ground containing 28 burials dated to the Etruscan period.

props.ancientName: Necropoli etrusca di San Germano (Gavorrano, GR)

name: Tomb of the Mayor of Athribis
description: The tomb of Psenosiris, identified in a tomb inscription as the Mayor of Athribis, is a rock-cut shaft tomb with a richly-decorated burial chamber. The tomb was constructed in the necropolis of Athribis in the middle of the first century CE (ca. 37-54 CE).

name: Madinat Hamad 3 Burial Mound Field (Dar Kulayb)
description: A necropolis of the early Dilmun culture, dating to the second millennium B.C.

name: Costone della Folonia necropolis
description: The Costone della Folonia necropolis of Sovana.

name: Valle Bona-Monte Rosello necropolis
description: A Hellenistic necropolis of Suana.

name: Saite-Persian cemetery at Abu Sir
description: At Abu Sir, a cemetery of tombs from the Saite period: is located directly south of the pyramid of Neferefre.

name: Tomb of Marcus Antonius Antius Lupus
description: The Tomb of Marcus Antonius Antius Lupus is located at the third mile of the via Ostiensis.

props.ancientName: Tomb of Marcus Antonius Antius Lupus

name: Pyramid of Ameny Qemau
description: Pyramid of King Ameny Qemau located in Dahshur.

name: Via Basiliano necropolis
description: An ancient necropolis located in the modern Parco della Serenissima that was initially discovered via construction works in 1997. The necropolis is located, in ancient terms, in a landscape between the line of the via Collatina and that of the via Praenestina. The excavations also revealed traces of first-millennium BCE agricultural activity.

name: Pyramid of Sekhemkhet
description: The Pyramid of Sekhemkhet, also known as "The Buried Pyramid", is an unfinished step pyramid at Saqqara that was begun ca. 2645 B.C. for Sekhemkhet Djoserty.

name: Pyramid of Khendjer
description: The pyramid of the Pharaoh Khendjer (Thirteenth Dynasty) who ruled Egypt during the Second Intermediate Period (ca. 1760 B.C.)

name: Mastabet el-Fara'un
description: The site of the burial of Shepseskaf, the last known pharaoh of the Fourth Dynasty of Ancient Egypt.

name: I͡Asinovskiĭ III Kurgan
description: Bronze age burial site near the banks of the Seversky Donets.

props.ancientName: I͡Asinovskiĭ III kurgan, Iasinovskii III kurgan, Yasinovskiy III kurgan
