name: Mugharet Abu Halimeh
description: Cave tomb near the modern village of Silat aḑ-Ḑahr containing burials from the Roman and Byzantine periods (1st-early 7th century CE). The cave is named after the Abu Halimeh family, which owned the land on which the cave is located at the time of its excavation in 1949.

name: Casale Vignale necropolis
description: An Etruscan rock-cut necropolis of San Giovenale.

name: Casal Bertone necropolis
description: Casal Bertone is named for the modern Roman neighborhood where it was discovered, about 2 km east of the Porta Maggiore. The site of Casal Bertone covers more than 3,000 square meters. The eastern portion of the site comprised a residential area that was occupied from about the first century B.C. to the fifth to sixth centuries A.D.

name: 'Awām
description: The ruins of the Sabaean temple dedicated to Almaqah and its associated burial ground are located near the town of Mārib, Yemen.

name: Al-'Assāsīf
description: Necropolis on the West Bank of the Nile near Thebes containing tombs from the 18th, 25th, and 26th dynasties.

props.ancientName: Al-'Assāsīf, El-Assasif, Al-Assaif, Assasif, Asasif, El-Asasif, Al-Asasif

name: Necropoli de La Petrina
description: The "La Petrina" necropolis is associated with ancient Narce and was in use from the eighth to the third centuries BCE.

name: Necropolis of San Giuseppe
description: The Necropolis of San Giuseppe is located along a cut tufo road or via cava near Pitigliano.

name: Abusir South
description: Abusir South, located north of Saqqara, refers to an archaeological area where a large cemetery of lower-ranking officials of the Old Kingdom has been identified.

name: Etruscan necropolis of Malignano
description: An Etruscan necropolis south of Sovicille in the province of Siena. The tombs date from the fifth century B.C. onward.

name: Etruscan necropolis of Pianacce
description: An Etruscan necropolis located near the center of Sarteano with tombs dating from the sixth to the second centuries B.C. In 2003, the fourth-century painted tomb of the 'infernal quadriga' was discovered.

name: Vatican necropolis
description: The Vatican necropolis lies beneath Vatican City, specifically beneath the present St. Peter's Basilica, at depths ranging from 5 to 12 meters. The necropolis was partially exposed by means of archaeological excavations in 1940-1949. The necropolis was an open-air burial ground that included both mausolea and tombs. Notable among the documented tombs are the Tomb of the Iulii and the tomb that is conventionally believed to be that of Saint Peter (a.k.a. Peter the Apostle). The tomb of the saint, said to be located near the site of his martyrdom, provides a point of topographic orientation for the Constantinian project that came to be Old St. Peter's. The necropolis remained in use until at least 318.

name: Tomb of Servius Sulpicius Galba
description: A tomb of the late second century BCE originally located near the Via Ostensis in the district of Rome now known as Testaccio. In antiquity, this was the area known as the Emporium. The tomb is visible on a fragment of the Severan marble plan of Rome and it lies between the Horrea Galbana and the “Porticus Aemilia”. It was rediscovered in 1885 and reconstructed on the grounds of the municipal antiquarium on the Caelian Hill. The tomb belongs to a Servius Sulpicius Galba and is likely to be that of either the consul of 144 BCE or the consul of 108 BCE.

name: Tomb of Marcus Alleius Minius
description: The Tomb of Marcus Alleius Minius is located outside the Porta Stabia of Pompeii. It is a schola-type tomb, excavated 1888-1890. The tomb carries a Latin inscription and it was buried by debris following the 62 CE earthquake.

name: Sepulcra Salaria
description: The area known as "Sepulcra Salaria" corresponds to the current-day Piazza Fiume, a public square adjacent to the intersection of the Corso d'Italia and Via Piave. Beginning in the later republican period, this funereal area developed. A range of tomb typologies has been identified here.

name: Koumasa prepalatial cemetery
description: A prepalatial cemetery on the island of Crete that includes three tholos tombs and one rectangular tomb.

name: Necropolis of Sant'Andrea Priu
description: A prehistoric necropolis in Sardinia associated with the Ozieri culture and dating to the Final Neolithic period (ca. 3500-2900 BC).

name: Canicella necropolis
description: An Etruscan necropolis located to the south of the city of Orvieto with tombs ranging in date from the eighth to the third centuries B.C.

name: Tomb of Horemheb (KV57)
description: Horemheb, final ruler of the 18th dynasty, was buried in KV57.

name: Troculu
description: A megalithic "giant's tomb" in proximity to a nuragic settlement.

name: Tiszapolgár-Basatanya
description: A Chalcolithic cemetery located at modern Polgár, Hungary, with at least 156 graves. This site is the typesite for the Tiszapolgár or Tiszapolgár-Româneşti culture, an Eneolithic archaeological culture. This archaeological culture has been identified on the Great Hungarian Plain and elsewhere in eastern, central Europe. It dates ca. 4500-4000 BCE.

name: François Tomb
description: An important painted Etruscan tomb dating to the final quarter of the fourth century B.C.

name: Vagnari
description: A villa, vicus, and necropolis site in modern Puglia, Italy, located near the modern city of Gravina. The site was active from the first to fourth centuries CE.

name: Byzantine necropolis
description: A necropolis of Scolacium dating to the fifth through seventh centuries.

name: Drususstein (Drususstone) at Mogontiacum
description: The remains of a Roman monument at Mainz, perhaps a cenotaph erected in 9 B.C.

name: Columbarium, Via del Casale Ferranti
description: Roman columbarium tombs in the Via del Casale Ferranti.

name: Tomb of Lison and Kallikles
description: A painted Macedonian tomb located at Lefkadia, Imathia, Macedonia, that dates to the third century BCE.

name: Crocifisso del Tufo necropolis
description: A major necropolis of Volsinii that reaches its maximum extent between 550 and 450 BC. The necropolis is especially noteworthy on account of its orthogonal layout.

name: Tomba dei giganti di Laccaneddu
description: A Bronze Age "giant's tomb" in Sardinia.

name: Horoztepe
description: Early Bronze Age mound located near the town of Erbaa in Northern Anatolia.

name: Myskhako necropolis
description: A burial area on the Black Sea coast that is associated with the settlement of Myskhako. In 1978-1979 excavations were conducted that uncovered 81 burial sites dating to the Bronze Age, the Classical period, and the Middle Ages.

name: Columbarium of Tiberius Claudius Vitalis
description: A columbarium tomb located on the grounds of the Villa Wolkonsky in Rome, Italy.

name: Pyramid of Menkaure
description: The Pyramid of Menkaure is the smallest of the three main pyramids located on the Giza plateau. It marks the tomb of the fourth dynasty Egyptian Pharaoh Menkaure.

name: Tomb of Hetepheres I
description: Hetepheres I was a 4th Dynasty queen of ancient Egypt (ca. 2600 B.C). She may have been a wife of the pharaoh Sneferu and the mother of Khufu.

name: Tamiryn Ulaan Khoshuu
description: Burial site located near the northern bank of the Tamir River in Mongolia. The site contains nearly 300 burials dated to the Xiongnu period (ca. 3rd century BCE-1st century CE).

name: Tytkesken-VI
description: Multi-period burial site in the northern Altai region. The bulk of the burials on the site are dated to the Pazyryk period (6th-4th centuries BCE), with additional burials from the Byikenskaya (8th-6th centuries BCE), Bulan-Kobinskaya (2nd-3rd centuries CE), and Turkic (6th-8th centuries CE) cultures.

name: Abu Bakr cemetery
description: Burial area in the northwestern portion of the Giza Necropolis containing burials dated to the Fourth-Fifth Dynasties.

name: Roman necropolis at Melano
description: Roman necropolis in the modern town of Melano, Switzerland, containing 29 burials dated to the 1st-2nd centuries CE.

name: Santa Savinilla catacomb
description: A Late Imperial catacomb located in the territory of Nepet. The Christian church of San Tolomeo alle Sante Grotte developed at the site of the catacomb.

name: La Côte-St-André
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 E3 La Côte-St-André

name: Arzhan-2
description: An early Scythian burial mound located in what is now the Tuva Republic of Russia.

name: Cemetery of the Garamantes
description: Unnamed cemetery in the el-Agial escarpment

name: La Celsa
description: At La Celsa (12.7 km of the Via Flaminia) are the remains of a cylindrical drum mausoleum that was likely once part of a larger funereal complex.

name: Archaic tholos tomb at Pharsalos
description: Center point of Archaic period tholos tomb of Pharsalos, used until the Hellenistic period.

props.ancientName: Tholotos taphos Pharslon, Tholotós táphos Pharsálon

name: Ashen Hill Barrows
description: The Ashen Hill Barrow Cemetery is located in Somerset, England. These barrows are the most northerly of the sites in this area. The cemetery consists of six bowl barrows and two bell barrows that are aligned on an east-to-west axis.

name: Robin Hood's Butts
description: A group of nine Bronze Age barrows in Somerset, England.

name: Necropoli Fondo Scataglini
description: An Etruscan necropolis at Tarquinia with more than 175 tombs.

name: Glyfa
description: A Middle Helladic necropolis excavated to the west of Fanos, Phthiotis, Greece.

name: Tomb of Kar (Megarid)
description: A tower at Megara was thought to be the tomb of the legendary Kar, son of Phoroneus.

props.ancientName: Karos tou Phoroneus mnema, Karòs tou Phoroneus mnemá

name: Necropolis of Porta Ercolano
description: The Porta Ercolano necropolis, excavated between 1763 and 1838, is the best known of Pompeii's necropoleis, in use from the middle of the first century BC until the first century AD.

name: Arc de Campanus
description: The so-called Arc de Campanus is either a funerary monument or a monumental arch dating to the early Roman Imperial period.

name: Etruscan Pyramid of Bomarzo
description: The so-called Etruscan Pyramid of Bomarzo is a megalithic, rock-cut monument at Bomarzo. It likely dates to the late Archaic period.

name: Tomb of Marcus Veius Marcellus
description: The Tomb of Marcus Veius Marcellus at Pompeii.

name: Tomb of the Ottavii
description: The mid-third century CE tomb of the Ottavii was discovered beneath the Villino Cardani (now at Via della Stazione di Ottavia, 73) on 17 August 1921. Based on epigraphic evidence, the tomb was occupied by the remains of one Octavia Paolina, her father Octavius Felix, and two other relatives. The archaeological remains of a number of ancient villas are known in the area, mostly lying on property that later belonged to the Colonna family.

name: Tomb of Lucius Verginius Rufus
description: The Tomb of Lucius Verginius Rufus was located at ancient Alsium. Lucius Verginius Rufus (d. 97 CE) was a colleague of Nero and guardian of the younger Pliny, with the latter recording Verginius Rufus' epitaph.

name: Grabbau 79 (Tuna el-Gebel)
description: A monumental burial structure in the so-called "Petosiris-Nekropole" at Tuna el-Gebel, excavated in 2021. Immediately north of the tomb, in the street area, stone basins supporting undisturbed lead coffins, as well as other associated burials, were found in situ.

name: Tomb of the Tassinara
description: A painted Etruscan tomb at Chiusi that dates ca. 170-150 BCE. The tomb has acquired a constellation of modern appellations, including "tomba della Tassinaie", "tomba della Tassinara," and "tomba della Tassinaia".

name: Son Real necropolis
description: A prehistoric necropolis near punta Fenicis that remained in use through the Roman period.

name: Qidfaʻ 1
description: Site of a 2nd millennium BCE tomb located in Qidfaʻ, Fujairah Emirate, United Arab Emirates. The site was damaged by farming in the area.

name: Grand mausolée des Flavii
description: A mausoleum near Cillium dates to the second century CE. Tiberius Flavius Secundus built the family tomb, dedicating it to his father, and inscribing two poetic texts totaling some 110 lines on its facade. Located in what is now Kasserine, Tunisia.

name: Tomb of Numerius Agrestinus Equitius Pulcher
description: A schola tomb at Pompeii located near the Porta Nocera necropolis. The inscription on the tomb's hemicycle honors Numerius Agrestinus Equitius Pulcher, a Roman who had an illustrious military career, including holding the role of praefectus of the Autrigones, an Iberian tribe.

name: Puig des Molins necropolis
description: A Carthaginian necropolis on the island of Ibiza.

name: Contrada Diana
description: A prehistoric settlement and Greco-Roman era necropolis underlying the modern city of Lipari, with burials dating from the bronze age through the Roman period.

name: Monte Luna Necropoleis
description: Two adjacent funerary landscapes located south of modern Senorbì on the island of Sardinia, with burials ranging from the 6th century BCE to the 6th century CE.

name: Tomb of Hilarus Fuscus
description: The Late Republican tomb of the freedman Hilarus Fuscus along the Via Appia (ca. 30 B.C.).

name: Tomb of Rekhmire at Thebes
description: Theban Tomb 100 (TT100), the burial place of Rekhmire, a noble of the 18th dynasty.

name: Crocodile Necropolis
description: Cave approximately 17km Northwest of El Maabdeh, Egypt where thousands of mummified crocodiles were found in addition to several other types of animals and human mummies as part of the Egyptian crocodile cult. All discoveries at this site were from the Ptolemaic or Roman periods.

name: Tabriz: Iron Age Cemetery
description: In 1997, during work to restore and consolidate the "Blue Mosque" in modern Tabriz, Iran, an extensive Iron Age cemetery was discovered and excavated. Since 2007, the site has been partly open to the public as an open-air museum.

name: Deir el-Bahari
description: Deir el-Bahari is a complex of mortuary temples and tombs that constitute a part of the Theban necropolis located on the west bank of the Nile river, opposite Luxor.

name: Theban Tomb 69
description: TT69 is located in the Theban Necropolis on the west bank of the Nile. It served as the tomb of an Egyptian official known as Menna, among whose titles were "Overseer of Fields of Amun", and "Overseer of Fields of the Lord of the Two Lands". The tomb is generally dated to the reign of Thutmosis IV.

name: Tomb of Derecia Methe
description: The Tomb of Derecia Methe is a house-type tomb located at the Porta Nocera.

name: Tomb of Annedia and Lucius Caesius
description: The Tomb of Annedia and Lucius Caesius is an aedicula tomb in the Porta Nocera necropolis.

name: Mausoleum of C. Ennius Marsus
description: C. Ennius Marsus was duovir at Saepinum.

name: Tomb of Mereruka
description: The mastaba tomb of the vizier Mereruka at Saqqarah dates to the Sixth Dynasty of ancient Egypt.

name: Berretta del Prete
description: A circular Roman mausoleum with a distinctive cupola located at the ninth mile of the Via Appia.

name: Tell Aḥmad al-Ḥattū
description: Early dynastic necropolis in southern Mesopotamia. The site was inundated by the creation of Lake Hamrin in 1981.

name: Vounous Cemetery (Cyprus)
description: Cemetery site in northern Cyprus near Kyrenia with Bronze Age remains.

name: Klady
description: Eneolithic and early Bronze Age burial ground in the Northwest Caucasus with about 40 mounds located 3km northwest of Tsarskaya.

name: Tomb of Marcus Servilius Quartus
description: A Roman tomb on the Via Appia dating ca. A.D. 100.

name: Tomb Garden of Aur(elius) Inachus
description: An inscription found beside the Via Appia near the tomb of Caecilia Metella in 1851 identifies a cepotaphium, i.e., a tomb in a garden, belonging to one Aur(elius) Inachus.

name: Hellenistic Funerary Monument (Keryneia)
description: Also frequently referred to as "the Heroon" in archaeological literature, this structure at the site of Keryneia in Achaea was excavated in the mid-20th century.

name: Necropoli di Montarano
description: A necropolis located to the north of ancient Falerii Veteres containing more than 67 tombs.

name: Mattonara necropolis
description: The Etruscan necropolis of La Mattonara near Civitavecchia, Italy.

name: Shiveet khaĭrkhany
description: Burial site in western Mongolia that dates to between the 8th century BCE and the 3rd century CE. Nine tombs associated with the Pazyryk Culture and 7 tombs associated with the Xianbei Culture have been identified.

name: Salkhityn Am
description: Cemetery site in northern Mongolia containing 35 burials dated to the Early Xiongnu period (200-50 BCE).

name: Phoenician cemetery at al-Bass
description: Cemetery at the Al-Bass site in Tyre containing burials, funerary urns, and stelae dated to the Phoenician period (8th-6th century BCE).

name: Roman necropolis at al-Bass
description: The Tyre necropolis, part of the UNESCO World Heritage site of Tyre, contains a triumphal arch and hundreds of stone sarcophagi dated to the Roman and Byzantine periods. It is located adjacent to an older Phoenician burial site.

name: Asomata
description: Burial site located southeast of Mount Vermion. The site was primarily used during the Archaic period (mid 6th-early 5th centuries BCE), with additional burials through the late Classical-early Hellenistic period and some additional signs of earlier human activity from the Middle Neolithic period through the Bronze Age. Rescue excavations were conducted on the site in 2000-2002 during the construction of the Egnatia Highway.

name: Ketef Hinnom
description: Cave tomb complex located southwest of Jerusalem containing burials dated to the 6th century BCE.

props.ancientName: Ketef Hinnom, Katef Hinom, Ketef Hinom

name: Pyramid K.1 at El-Kurru
description: The oldest tomb at El-Kurru.

name: Catacombs of Praetextatus
description: A vast Roman catacomb along the Via Appia that began to be used at the close of the second century A.D.

name: Vigna Pia necropolis
description: An imperial necropolis with columbarium tombs and a family tomb connected with the gens Atilia Romana.

name: Makronissos tombs
description: Burial site in southern Cyprus containing burials dated to the Hellenistic and Roman periods.

name: Marlik
description: Burial site in northern Iran containing over fifty burials dated to the mid-2nd millennium BCE.

name: Tomb of Nebamun at Thebes
description: Theban Tomb no. 65 (TT65), the tomb of Nebamun, dated to the reign of Hatshepsut. The tomb was reused in the Twentieth Dynasty for Imiseba, a member of the Theban Clergy. In late antiquity it (along with several of the surrounding tombs) was repurposed as an anchorite dwelling, called in modern times the "Monastery of Cyriacus."

name: Tomb of the Sempronii (Palazzo San Felice, Rome)
description: A late-Republican tomb located on the slope of the Quirinal Hill at Rome, now built into Palazzo San Felice (via della Dataria 21).

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 C1 unnamed unlabeled ((5 monuments, 2 cemeteries))

name: Casacce necropolis
description: A rock-cut necropolis at Blera with tombs of the Hellenistic period.

name: Torremocha
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D5 Torremocha

name: Monument La Torratxa (Vilablareix)
description: A Roman funerary monument located at modern Vilablareix, southwest of Girona, in Catalonia.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F2 no. 47 (Puységur)

name: Mausoleum of Lucius Aemilius Lupus
description: A Roman tomb of one Lucius Aemilius Lupus.

name: La Sinagoga mausoleum and villa
description: A Roman mausoleum and villa of the fourth century AD in the locality known as La Sinagoga near Sádaba, Spain.

name: Voudeni Cemetery
description: The Voudeni Cemetery is a Mycenaean cemetery in Achaea, Greece, that was used from ca. 1500-1050 BCE. The archaeological exploration of the site began in 1923 under Nikolaos Kyparissis.

name: Char Bakr
description: Necropolis of the Jūybāri shaikhs located approximately 5 km west of Bukhara. The complex was constructed during the 16th century around the tomb of the scholar Emām Abu Bakr Saʿd.

name: Mausoleum at Halicarnassus
description: The Mausoleum at Halicarnassus is a tomb built between 353 and 350 B.C. for Mausolus, a satrap in the Persian Empire. Antipater of Sidon identified it as one of his Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.

name: St-Jean-d’Arves
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 G3 St-Jean-d’Arves

name: Fürst
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E1 Fürst

name: Aukštakiemiai, Palanga, Šernai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 H2 Aukštakiemiai, Palanga, Šernai

name: Enekrogen, Kannikegård, Slamrebjerg, Slusegård
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 G2 Enekrogen, Kannikegård, Slamrebjerg, Slusegård

name: Lilla Jored
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 F2 Lilla Jored

name: Lugii
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 G3 Lugii

name: Odry
description: The modern Polish city of Odry is the site of a major "stone circles" cemetery of the iron age Wielbark Culture.

name: Öremölla
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 F2 Öremölla

name: Store-Dal
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 2 F2 Store-Dal

name: Prosymna tholos
description: A Mycenaean tholos tomb.

name: Fondo Pacifico necropolis
description: A necropolis of Pompeii. The eastern sector was excavated in 1886-7 and is now reinterred.

name: Tomb of the Lollii
description: The Tomb of the freedman Marcus Lollius Lucrio and his familia at Pompeii.

name: Tomb of the Blaesii
description: North Tomb H in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.

name: Unnamed tomb
description: An unnamed tomb, funeral enclosure, or perhaps ustrinum in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.

name: Mausoleum of Gallienus
description: A mausoleum on the Via Appia that has been identified conventionally as that of the emperor Gallienus.

name: Tombs of the Festoons and Frontespizio
description: Two collocated tombs on the Via Appia - a Republican tomb belonging to the time of Sulla known as the 'Tombs of the Festoons' and a second tomb called the 'Tomb of the Title Page' by Luigi Canina who studied and restored it.

name: Constantinian Columbarium
description: The so-called Constantinian Columbarium is actually a second-century AD tomb on the Via Appia.

name: Tomb of Gaius Rabirius Postumus Libertus and family
description: A tomb of the Republican period belonging to the freedman family of the Rabirii.

name: Tholos de El Romeral
description: A megalithic tomb dating ca. 1800 BC located in Antequera (Andalusia), Spain.

name: Naveta des Tudons
description: A chamber tomb of the late second to early first millennium BCE located on the island of Minorca.

name: La Salata necropolis
description: An early Christian necropolis with tombs dating to the fourth through sixth centuries CE in the Italian comune of Vieste.

name: Zawyet el-Maiyitin
description: A small pyramid of the late Third Dynasty of ancient Egypt.

name: Torrione di Micara
description: A Roman tomb that was adapted for use as a medieval fortification. It is located near modern Frascati, southeast of Rome.

name: Aleksandrovo tumulus
description: The Aleksandrovo tumulus or kurgan is a Thracian burial mound located near Aleksandrovo in south-eastern Bulgaria, that dates to the fourth century BC.

name: Bronze Age Cemetery at Mursia
description: A cemetery (referred to collectively in Italian as "sesi") consisting of multi-burial stone cairns associated with the Bronze Age settlement at Mursia on Mediterranean island of Pantelleria.

name: Tomb of Menidi
description: The Tomb of Menidi is a Mycenaean tholos tomb first discovered in 1872. It is one of four such tholoi known in Attica.

name: A'ali Mound Cemetery
description: A large complex of burial mounds located near the town of A'ali in central Bahrain. The burials date to the early Dilmun era, ca. 2050-1700 BCE.

name: Torrione di Poli
description: A Republican-period tower tomb located in the Comune di Poli in Lazio, Italy.

name: White Pyramid
description: A pyramid built by Pharaoh Amenemhat II.

name: Hypogeum of via Dino Compagni
description: A Christian catacomb on the ancient Via Latina used during the fourth century.

name: Tomb of Puimre at Thebes
description: Tomb TT39 in the Theban Necropolis, the burial site of Puimre (or Puyemre), an Egyptian official of the 18th Dynasty.

name: Tophet of Carthage
description: The Tophet of Carthage, also known as tophet de Salammbô, is a hybrid sanctuary-necropolis of Punic Carthage.

name: Montefortini
description: A monumental Etruscan tumulus that covers two chamber tombs that were looted in antiquity but that likely dated to the seventh and sixth centuries B.C.

name: Lori Berd necropolis
description: Burial site in Armenia containing tombs dated to the Late Bronze-Early Iron Ages (22nd-5th centuries BCE).

name: Barsova Gora
description: Archaeological site on the Ob River in Khanty-Mansi Autonomous Okrug, Russia containing remains dated to the 6th-4th centuries BCE.

props.ancientName: Barsova Gora, Barsovoy Gore, Barsovoĭ Gore, Barsovoĭ Gora

name: Tumulus of Noville
description: A Gallo-Roman tumulus near Noville, Belgium.

name: Tumuli of Koninksem
description: A pair of Gallo-Roman tumuli dating to the first through third centuries AD.

name: Tumulus of Havelange
description: The Tumulus of Havelange is a Gallo-Roman burial mound located at Havelange, Belgium.

name: Tumulus of Vissoul
description: A Gallo-Roman burial mound located near Vissoul in Belgium.

name: Necropolis of Selva Miccia
description: The Necropolis of Selva Miccia, located along the road leading to Vulci, contains tombs of the seventh and sixth centuries B.C., corresponding in phase to the elite structure excavated at località Le Sparne.

name: Tomba de Tre Camini
description: An ancient tomb located along the via di Terrano on the outskirts of Falerii Novi.

name: Audleystown Court Cairn
description: A dual court grave near the south shore of Strangford Lough in County Down, Northern Ireland.

name: Cloghanmore
description: A megalithic chamber tomb located in County Donegal, Ireland.

name: Knockeen dolmen
description: A megalithic dolmen located in Knockeen, County Waterford, Ireland.

name: Hottomont tumulus
description: A Gallo-Roman tumulus near Ramillies in Belgium.

name: Tumulus of Marcinelle
description: A Gallo-Roman tumulus located nesr Marcinelle Charleroi in Belgium.

name: Libersart
description: A pair of Gallo-Roman burial mounds are located at Libersart in Belgium. The tombs may relate to a second and third-century AD vicus in the area.

name: Plattetombe of Waasmont
description: The Plattetombe (flat tomb) is a Gallo-Roman burial bound of the second century AD.

name: Seila Pyramid
description: A pyramid, originally approximately 23m in height, built by the fourth dynasty pharaoh Snefru. The function of the pyramid, which seems to have lacked an interior chamber, is uncertain.

name: Santa Costanza
description: A fourth century AD structure along the Via Nomentana that was built by Constantine I as a mausoleum, originally housing the remains of his daughters Constantina and Helena. It was dedicated as a Christian church in the Middle Ages.

name: Oakley Down
description: A Bronze Age barrow cemetery.

name: Komiaki Mycenaean Tholos Tomb
description: A Mycenaean Tholos Tomb dating to the middle of the second millennium BCE located to the north of Koronida/Komiaki on the island of Naxos in the Aegean Sea.

name: Necropoli di San Placido
description: A large Roman necropolis in Messina, uncovered in 1914 during the works to build the modern Palazzo della Prefettura.

name: Rouweyha
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of an ancient basilica, residential buildings, and several tombs. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Coddu Vecchiu
description: A Bronze Age megalithic gallery grave on Sardinia that belongs to the "giants' tomb" classification.

name: Dibba
description: Iron age tomb where cuneiform inscription was found.

name: Issyk kurgan field
description: Saka burial ground in modern Issyk, Kazakhstan, containing over 40 kurgans dated from the 4th-3rd centuries BCE. The primary kurgan contained the burial of a richly-dressed individual now known as the "Golden Man."

name: Porta Nocera necropolis
description: The Porta Nocera necropolis at Pompeii.

name: Torre Dell'Angelo
description: A Roman columbarium tomb located on the Via Latina.

name: Via Celle necropolis
description: A Roman necropolis with 14 tombs constructed during the first and early second centuries A.D.

name: Da u Dukhtar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 B3 Da u Dukhtar

name: Naqsh-i Rustam
description: An ancient necropolis located northwest of Persepolis.

name: al-Hajjar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 95 C3 al-Hajjar

name: Columbarium of Pomponius Hylas
description: A Roman columbarium on the Via Appia close to the Porta Latina. The columbarium was excavated in 1831 by Pietro Campana.

name: El Qattah
description: At this site located northwest of modern Cairo in Egypt's Giza governate, early twentieth-century excavations identified, among other finds, a Middle Kingdom necropolis with evidence of at least 14 tombs, including the tomb of one Neha, a Middle Kingdom prince.

name: Funerary chapel of Shepenupet II, Nitocris I and Mehytenweskhet
description: Funerary chapel of the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Shepenupet II, daughter of King Piankhi. Enlarged by her successor, the Divine Adoratrice of Amun Nitocris I, daughter of King Psamtik I, to accommodate her cult and that of her mother Mehytenweskhet.

props.ancientName: Funerary chapel of Shepenupet II, Nitocris I and Mehytenweskhet

name: Apesokari Tholos tomb B
description: Minoan tomb in the Mesara Plain in south-central Crete constructed during the Early Minoan I period (ca. 3100-2650 BCE) and expanded later in the Prepalatial and Protopalatial periods (through 1700 BCE).

name: Kastros
description: Burial site in the modern town of Lapithos in northern Cyprus containing burials dated to the Geometric Period.

name: Saint-Michel tumulus
description: The Saint-Michel tumulus is a megalithic funerary mound in Brittany and one of the largest such mounds in Europe.

name: Brownshill Dolmen
description: The Brownshill Dolmen is a megalithic portal tomb built between 4000 and 3000 BC near Carlow, Ireland.

name: Khisht tepe
description: Khush Tepe, also known as Tepe Fullol, is a burial site associated with the Oxus Civilization. Discovered in 1966, the site contained gold and silver vessels dated to the Bronze Age (ca. 2600-1700 BCE).

name: Grommern
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 10 H4 Grommern

name: Westerwanna
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 10 E3 Westerwanna

name: So-called 'Tomb of Cicero' at Formiae
description: A 24-meter tall tower tomb on the Via Appia at Formiae is traditionally considered as a possible location of the Tomb of Cicero.

name: Pizzone necropolis
description: A Roman necropolis located at Nocera Superiore which was buried by the volcanic ejecta of the Vesuvian eruption of A.D. 79.

name: Street of Facades, Petra
description: The so-called "Street of Facades" is a necropolis that is lined with tall, rock-cut tombs that have elaborate facades.

name: Bougon
description: Bougon is the site of five Neolithic barrows located in Poitou-Charentes, France. The earliest dates ca. 4800 BC.

name: Necropolis of Campo della Fonte
description: The Campo della Fonte at Rusellae.

name: Località De Santis I at Pontecagnano
description: Necropolis in Pontecagnano containing fifty burials from the Orientalizing Period (725-550 BCE) and an additional six burials from the Lucanian period (4th-3rd centuries BCE).

name: S'Altare de Logula
description: The Tomb of the Giants Sa Perda 'e S'Altare is a giants' tomb located in the municipality of Birori, province of Nuoro, Sardinia.

name: Monte Aguzzo tumulus
description: Monte Aguzzo di Formello (Rome), the site of a tumulus along the road leading for Veii to the Ager Faliscus.

name: Mausoleum Augusti
description: A monumental tomb built in the Campus Martius at Rome by Augustus and dedicated in 28 B.C. The mausoleum served as a family tomb for the Iulio-Claudians.

name: Tomb of Artaxerxes III
description: The tomb is traditionally associated with the Persian king Artaxerxes III Ochus (358-338 BC).

name: Sasso Pinzuto necropolis
description: The Sasso Pinzuto necropolis is linked with the Etruscan culture and is located near Tuscania, Italy. The necropolis was in use from the middle of the seventh century BCE until the fifth century BCE. A new cult area was discovered at Sasso Pinzuto in 2024.

name: Ara del Tufo necropolis
description: An Etruscan necropolis at Tuscania.

name: Pini di Sirolo
description: "I Pini di Sirolo" is the name given to the archaeological site of a Picene necropolis of the first millennium BCE in Italy's Marche region. The necropolis included a large tumulus of the sixth century BCE that included two chariots.

name: Frontovoe 3 Cemetery
description: Roman-era cemetery in the Crimea with 332 burial structures containing 500 bodies and 20,000 artifacts. The site was destroyed with the creation of the Taurida Expressway.

name: Weissenfels
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 E1 Weissenfels

name: Měnín (cemetery)
description: An ancient cemetery, located at modern Měnín in the Czech Republic.

name: Opatów
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 13 D2 Opatów

name: Tarnów Opolski Roman Cemetery
description: A Roman cemetery at modern Tarnów Opolski in Poland.

name: Wrocław-Zakrzów
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 13 C1 Wrocław-Zakrzów

name: Les Cars
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H3 Les Cars

name: Cinq-Mars-la-Pile
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F1 Cinq-Mars-la-Pile

name: Abbatija tad-Dejr
description: A multi-period burial site located in the northwest of modern Ir-Rabat, Malta. Archaeological evidence points to first use of the site in the Punic and Roman periods. A quarry of possible Roman date was used in the construction of Byzantine-period hypogea. Further modifications were made in the late Medieval (post-Muslim) period, prior to abandonment of the site.

name: Monumenti Funerari Romani
description: An archaeological area at Avella includes a sector of the ancient monumental necropolis that was in use between the late Hellenistic period and the early imperial period. It lies along an extra-urban road axis. Remains of four Roman mausolea are presently visible.

name: Tomba di Tulliola
description: The so-called tomb of Tulliola is a Roman mausoleum located at Formiae. Situated about 100 meters from the so-called tomb of Cicero, this tomb is held to be that of Cicero's father.

name: Tomb of Mentuemhat (TT 34)
description: The largest private tomb in the Theban Necropolis, located in el-Assasif and containing the burial of a local ruler of Thebes during the 25th-26th Dynasties.

name: Mazor Mausoleum
description: Roman mausoleum dated to the 3rd century CE. In the early Muslim period, the site was used as a shrine to the Prophet John.

props.ancientName: Maqām an-Nebī Yaḥya, Maqam en-Neby Yahya

name: Pyramid of Khentkawes
description: Khentkawes I was a queen of the 4th dynasty of ancient Egypt. She may have been a daughter of the pharaoh Menkaure, and the wife of both kings Shepseskaf and Userkaf. Her tomb at Giza – LG100 – is located close to Menkaure's pyramid complex.

name: Saar al-Jisr Burial Field
description: Burial ground near the Dilmun temple at Saar also from the Dilmun culture.

name: Beyoba tumulus
description: Tumulus located approximately 13km southeast of Parion. The tomb contained two burials dated to 350-300 BCE.

name: Adro Vello
description: The remains of a Roman villa and necropolis.

name: Baba Hatim Ziyarat
description: The mausoleum of Baba Hatim dates to the eleventh and twelfth centuries. It is located near Imam Sahib, in Kunduz Province, Afghanistan.

name: Cat Stane
description: An irregular standing stone bearing a fragmentary Latin inscription, the Cat Stane is likely a Neolithic monument re-used for a burial of the fifth or sixth century AD.

name: Tomb of Marcus Venerius Secundio at the Porta Sarno (Pompeii)
description: A tomb dating to the first century CE, excavated in 2021 in the necropolis of Porta Sarno at Pompeii.

name: Barnavos, Chamber Tomb C1749
description: A chamber tomb was excavated to the west of ancient Nemea in 2002 and 2003. The robbed tomb was dated to the Late Helladic IIIA2 period (ca. 1390–1315 BC BCE).

name: Klin-Yar
description: A burial ground near the modern town of Kislovodsk in the North Caucasus. The site contains 270 burials associated with the Iron Age Koban culture and the early medieval Sarmatians and Alans.

name: Via Livenza hypogeum
description: A painted hypogeum tomb of the fourth century A.D. discovered in 1923 between the via Livenza and the via Po.

name: Kato Phournos Tomb
description: The Kato Phournos Tomb is one of the nine tholos, or "beehive", tombs found around the acropolis of Mycenae. It is thought to have been constructed between 1460 and 1400 BC.

name: Columbarium of Constantine
description: The so-called Columbarium of Constantine is a second-century tomb that was later repurposed as a mill.

name: Catacombs of Commodilla
description: A catacomb along the Via Ostiensis that contains, among other wall paintings, the earliest depiction of a bearded Jesus Christ.

name: Necropoli di San Vito
description: A Greek necropolis located at Puteoli.

name: Tomb of Thutmose III (KV34)
description: KV34 served as the tomb of 18th dynasty Pharaoh Thutmose III.

name: Pyramid of Unas
description: A pyramid at Saqqara built for Unas, the final ruler of Egypt's Fifth Dynasty.

name: Naveta of Sa Torreta
description: The Naveta of Sa Torreta located on Menorca.

name: Bolʹshoĭ Petropavlovskiĭ burial ground
description: Burial complex along a stretch of over 3 km of the northern bank of the Siniukha River, a stream in the Laba River valley (Krasnodar region). The necropolis contains over 200 individual burials dated to the Middle Bronze-Early Iron Ages (3rd millennium BCE-3rd century CE).

props.ancientName: Bolʹshoĭ Petropavlovskiĭ mogilʹnik, Bolʹshoy Petropavlovskiy mogilʹnik

name: el-Amrah
description: Predynastic and Early Dynastic burial site containing over 1,000 burials. It is the type-site of the Amratian culture.

props.ancientName: Al-ʿĀmirah, el-Amrah, el Amrah, el-ʿÂmra, el ʿÂmra

name: Tomb of Aulus Hirtius
description: A tomb located in Rome's Campus Martius. Its remains are now submerged in the channelized waters of the Euripus canal beneath the Palazzo della Cancelleria.

name: Roman necropolis at Carmo
description: A necropolis of the Roman period wherein most tombs date between the first century BC and the second century AD.

name: Carmine Roman tomb
description: A Roman tomb of the later second century incorporated into the former Convento del Carmine in Catania, Sicily.

name: Sepolcro del Casale di Gregna
description: A Roman tomb of the fourth century C.E. built in opus listatum.

props.ancientName: sepolcro presso il Casale di Gregna

name: Tomba della Mula
description: A tomb near Sesto Fiorentino, that dates to the seventh century B.C.

name: Tomba della Montagnola
description: A tholos-type tomb dating to the seventh century B.C.

name: Mycenaean necropolis, Elateia
description: A Mycenaean necropolis with elaborate chamber tombs located north of modern Elateia, Phthiotida, Greece.

name: S. Passera hypogeum
description: A hypogeum connected with the S. Passera complex.

name: Catacombe San Marcellino e Pietro
description: The Catacombs of Marcellinus and Peter lies some 3 km southeast of Rome along the ancient Via Labicana. They date to the fourth century A.D.

name: Novochigol'skie kurgan
description: Cemetery site located on the banks of the Chigla River, near the modern village of Novaya Chigla, Voronezh Oblast. The site contains 28 burials associated with the Srubnaya Culture (Late Bronze Age 1900-1200 BCE) and the Sarmatians (1st-2nd centuries CE).

name: Salamis sea-fight tumulus (Salamis)
description: The so-called Salamis sea-fight tumulus includes the remains of an ancient tumulus and burials of the Classical period.

name: Mausoleum and Sacred area of Hecatomnus
description: A mausoleum and sacred precinct associated with Hecatomnus of Mylasa, who was the satrap of Caria under Artaxerxes II (404–358 BC).

name: The Giza Necropolis
description: This complex of ancient monuments includes the three pyramid complexes known as the Great Pyramids, the massive sculpture known as the Great Sphinx, several cemeteries, a workers' village and an industrial complex.

name: Mausoleum of Menenius Agrippa
description: The so-called mausoleum of Menenius Agrippa stands along the Via Nomentana.

name: Cavalupo Necropolis (Vulci)
description: Excavated in the nineteenth century and only recently re-assessed, this cemetery is located east of Vulci itself, across the modern river Fiora from the ancient acropolis. The cemetery was in active use from the Villanovan period through Hellenistic times.

name: Mokraya Balka cemetery
description: Burial site in the North Caucasus associated with the Alanic culture, containing nearly 100 burials dated from the 5th-12th centuries CE.

name: Filippovka Burial Ground
description: An archaeological site with nine burial mounds, including an intact royal mound (kurgan), located in the Southern Ural Region of Russia dating from the 6th-4th centuries BCE.

name: Qubbet el-Hawa
description: Qubbet el-Hawa is a site of a group of rock cut tombs known as the Princes' Tomb on the west side of the Nile, opposite Aswan. The tombs mostly date to the Old Kingdom.

name: Castello di Corradino
description: A mausoleum dating to the Roman Imperial period located at Centuripe in Sicily.

name: Tombs of the Buccii and Melissaei
description: The Grave enclosure of the Buccii and the Melissaei located on the west side of Pompeii's Herculaneum Gate.

props.ancientName: Tomba dei Buccii e dei Melissaei

name: Pyramid of Djoser
description: The pyramid of the Third Dynasty pharaoh Djoser was built at Saqqara by architect Imhotep ca. 2267-2648 B.C.

name: Ipatovo-3 Kurgan
description: A Bronze Age kurgan site in the Stavropol region.

name: Stary Sad
description: Bronze and Iron Age burial ground in the Baraba forest-steppe containing 75 burials of the Pakhomovka culture (late second millennium BCE), plus additional burials from the Andronovo culture (ca. 2200-900 BCE) and Sargat culture (ca. 500-250 BCE).

name: Manoppello Roman Villa
description: A Late Republican villa in Abruzzo that later became a productive estate. The archaeological remains of the villa are located in the locality of Piano S. Maria Arabona in Manoppello, Italy.

name: Māhān
description: Tomb of Shah Nematollah Vali, find place for cuneiform inscription.

name: Tumulo del Principe etrusco
description: A seventh-century B.C. Etruscan tumulus is located near Pisa.

name: Sukhoe Ozero II
description: Cemetery site in Khakassia containing numerous burials associated with the Karasuk Culture (14th-8th centuries BCE). The site was inundated following the construction of the Krasnoyarsk Hydroelectric Power Plant.

name: Pellana (Mycenaean cemetery)
description: Mycenaean chamber tombs located to the north of Pellana in Laconia.

name: Vigna Randanini
description: A Jewish catacomb along the Via Appia discovered in 1859.

name: Msletten obelisk-shaped tomb
description: One of a pair of obelisk-shaped tombs located along the Wadi Nefud in Libya.

name: Kurhan Perep'yatykha
description: A large Scythian burial mound that dates to the seventh and sixth centuries B.C.

props.ancientName: Kurhan Perep'yatykha, Kurhan Perep'i͡atykha, Kurhan Perep'yatycha

name: Helwan necropolis
description: Burial site on the east bank of the Nile (opposite Saqqara) containing over 100 burials dated to the Early Dynastic and Old Kingdom periods.

name: Pyramid of Teti
description: The Pyramid of Teti is a smooth-sided pyramid situated in the pyramid field at Saqqara. Teti was the first king of the Sixth Dynasty of Ancient Egypt.

name: Tomba della Vaschetta
description: The so-called "Tomba della Vaschetta" is a Roman tomb located in the Portuense necropolis.

name: Drepanon Cemetery
description: An ancient cemetery at modern Drepano in Greece, uncovered in rescue excavations since the 1970s.

name: Ferrone Necropolis
description: An Etruscan necropolis located on a tufaceous plateau overlooking the valley of the Fosso della Lenta. The tombs in the necropolis date between the seventh and fifth centuries BCE.

name: Necropolis of Grottini di Rota
description: An Etruscan necropolis located in the middle of the Mignone river valley in the comune of Canale Monterano.

name: Cancellone necropolis
description: Rock-cut tombs located along the remains of a road dating as early as the sixth century B.C.

name: Necropolis of Santa Maria in Borraccia
description: A seventh- and sixth-century B.C. Etruscan necropolis located in the territory of Heba.

name: Tanella Angori
description: A circular Hellenistic tomb of the second century BC.

name: Tomb of C. Veranius Rufus
description: The tomb of the freedman of Caius Veranius Rufus and freedman Verania Clara in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis at Pompeii.

name: Unnamed tomb
description: An ancient Roman tomb located in the Fondo Pacifico necropolis of Pompeii.

name: Tombs of the Calventii
description: Burial ground of the gens Calventii in the Porta Nocera necropolis of Pompeii.

name: Fortetsa Tombs
description: A Late Minoan burial site just West of the Knossos Acropolis.

name: Ilıpınar Tumulus
description: Tumulus near İznik Lake in Anatolia with Neolithic, Late Chalcolithic, Early Bronze Age, and Early Byzantine remains.

name: Tomb (heroon) of King Perikles of Lycia
description: The monumental tomb of Perikles was erected in Limyra. The tomb takes the form of an Ionic Greek temple.

name: Lugnano
description: Lugnano in Teverina is a comune located in the Province of Terni in the modern Italian region of Umbria. It lies about 60 km south of Perugia and 25 km west of Terni. Archaeological investigation has documented important examples of villa culture as well as Late Antique funereal evidence.

name: Mola di Monte Gelato
description: An ancient settlement 30 km north of Rome that was excavated from 1986-90. An imperial villa existed on the site up until the third century AD.

name: Quinto Fiorentino
description: Quinto Fiorentino is a place located in the plain of the Arno River to the west of Florence. Its location corresponds to the fifth mile of the Roman road. The site is especially known for two Etruscan tholos-type tombs a thòlos, one of which is the Tomba della Mula, and the other the Tomba della Montagnola.

name: Casal Rotondo
description: A Roman tomb that supports a Medieval farmhouse (casale) at the sixth mile of the Via Appia. The tomb originally dates ca. 30 BC; it was a large, circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 35 m.

name: Cessati Spiriti
description: A ruined Roman mausoleum on the grounds of the Caffarella regional park. The so-called Cessati Spiriti mausoleum was once clad in travertine.

name: La Colombella
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 La Colombella

name: Marmenia
description: The Domus Marmeniae is a villa located on the Via Appia near the fourth milestone. The villa grounds hosted a pagan necropolis and tradition holds that the matrona, Marmenia, relocated the body of St. Urban to a mausoleum built on the grounds.

name: Pompeo
description: A villa attributed to Cnaeus Pompeius at Albano Laziale.

name: S. Callisto
description: A large catacomb on the Via Appia south of Rome notable especially for its so-called 'Crypt of the Popes'. The catacomb covers approximately 15 hectares.

name: S. Ermete
description: S. Ermete is the site of a Paleochristian basilica and catacomb; the catacombs are also referred to as the Catacombs of Bassilla, and both names occur in the documentary sources.

name: S. Felicita
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B2 S. Felicita

name: S. Ippolito
description: A catacomb connected with the Christian Saint Hippolytus of Rome along the Via Tiburtina.

name: S. Valentino
description: A Christian catacomb along the Via Flaminia dedicated to S. Valentino.

name: Mausoleum of Caecilia Metella
description: A tomb on the Via Appia south of Rome belonging to Caecilia Metella, a daughter of Metellus Creticus. She was born ca. 100 BC. In 1303, the tomb was incorporated into the Castrum Caetani, serving as the main tower of the fortification.

name: Tomba degli Orazi e Curiazi
description: A Roman tomb near Albano Laziale connected in tradition with the story of the contest between the Horatii and Curiatii.

name: Tomb of Vibius Marianus
description: An imperial Roman tomb of Publius Vibius Marianus, erroneously identified as the Tomba di Nerone (Tomb of Nero) by G. B. Piranesi.

name: Il Torraccio
description: A circular mausoleum dating to the Late Republican period along the Via Praenestina.

name: Torre del Monumento
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C4 Torre del Monumento

name: Sepulcrum Arruntiorum
description: The tomb of the extended family (familia) of Lucius Arruntius (consul 6 CE) was located in the Viale della Principessa Margherita, about 100 meters from the Porta Maggiore.

name: Via Triumphalis necropolis
description: A segment of an imperial necropolis preserved along a portion of a consular road on the Vatican Hill. The Via Triumphalis necropolis was uncovered during the construction of a parking structure in the Via del Pellegrino.

name: Kerma Necropolis
description: An extensive necropolis of the Kerma culture located on Sai Island.

name: Cabeza de S. Pedro
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 E3 Cabeza de S. Pedro

name: Mausoleum of the Atilii at Sádaba
description: The Mausoleum of the Atilii is a Roman mausoleum dating from the second or third centuries A.D. The ruins are located in the municipal territory of Sádaba, Aragon, eastern Spain.

name: Tour de la Chèvre d'Or
description: A Roman funerary monument.

name: Pian di Mola necropolis
description: A monumental rock-cut necropolis at Tuscania.

name: Necropolis of Madonna d'olivo
description: An Etruscan necropolis located about 2 km south-west of Tuscania that overlooks the valley of the Marta river. The necropolis takes its name from the adjacent Church of the Madonna dell’Olivo.

name: Tomba Torlonia
description: An Etruscan tumulus-type tomb in the Monte Abatone necropolis of Caere that dates ca. 320-200 BCE.

name: Baghuz
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 91 B3 Baghuz

name: Tomb of Seti I
description: Tomb KV17 in the Valley of Kings was the burial place of the 19th dynasty Pharaoh Seti I.

name: Torre de San Josep
description: A Roman tower tomb located northeast of Villajoyosa, Spain. It is also known as the Torre de Hércules.

name: Tomb of Lucius Ceius Serapio
description: The tholos-type Tomb of Lucius Ceius Serapio and his wife, Helvia, stands near the Porta Nocera at Pompeii.

name: Tomb of the Stronnii
description: The Tomb of the Stronnii at Pompeii is a monumental altar tomb that is flanked by lions carved in tuff.

name: Tomb of Lucius Publicius Syneros, Aebia Fausta, Lucius Aebius Aristo and Aebia Hilara
description: A partially enclosed house tomb.

name: Bronze Age Barrow at Old Winchester Hill
description: A number of Bronze Age barrow tombs are found within the general area of the Iron Age hill fort.

name: Giessen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 I2 Giessen

name: Igel
description: The village of Igel is known for its funerary monument of the Secundinii dating ca. A.D. 250.

name: Hassleben
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 D1 Hassleben

name: Kleinzerbst
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 F1 Kleinzerbst

name: Přešt'ovice
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 G3 Přešt'ovice

name: Prositz
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 G1 Prositz

name: Pantano di Grano necropolis
description: A necropolis in the territory of Veii.

name: Hordiivka necropolis
description: A Bronze Age burial site located near the modern town of Hordiivka on the southern Bug River.

name: Ikh Khatuu
description: Area in the Altai region of extreme western Mongolia containing a number of burials and other structures dated from the Bronze Age and the Turkic Period.

name: Pachia Ammos Minoan Cemetery
description: Minoan cemetery located on a sandy beach in use from very early times until the Late Minoan period.

name: Sokolova Mohyla
description: Burial mound of Sarmatian nobility from the 1st century CE in modern Mykolaiv Oblast, Ukraine with earlier remains from the Bronze Age. The mound has since been demolished by bulldozer.

name: Paradimi Neolithic Tomb
description: Neolithic tomb located 1km east of the modern village of Paradiimi in Northeastern Greece.

name: Chamahzi Mumah
description: Iron Age burial site located ni the Pusht-i Kuh region of Luristan.

name: Platyskinos Tholos Tomb
description: A late Minoan tholos tomb in the area of modern Platyskinos/Platyschino on Crete.

name: Maltepe Tumulus
description: The Maltepe Tumulus is located in Manole, Bulgaria. The tumulus measures 125 – 130 m in diameter and some 21 m in height.

name: Gul Khanan Murdah
description: Iron Age cemetery site located west of modern Eyvanki in Iran.

name: Catacombe Ad Decimum
description: The Catacombs "Ad Decimum" are located at the tenth mile of the ancient Via Latina. This position corresponds roughly to the sixth kilometer of the present-day via Anagnina. The catacomb is well-preserved with roughly one thousand graves dating from the second to the fifth centuries CE.

name: Bryn Celli Ddu
description: A Neolithic mound site on the island of Anglesey.

name: Dyurso burial ground
description: A burial site in Krasnodar Krai, Russia, containing over 500 burials dated to the 5th-9th centuries CE.

props.ancientName: Mogilʹnik Di͡urso, Mogilʹnik Durso, Mogilʹnik Dyurso

name: Fontager necropolis
description: A necropolis in Gallia Narbonensis discovered within the thirteenth century Chateau de Fontager in 1963-1964.

name: Choburak-I
description: Cemetery site in the Altai Mountains containing burials associated with the Bulan-Koba culture (4th century CE).

name: Mavişehir Archaic Necropolis
description: An extensive archaic necropolis located on the eastern edge of modern Mavişehir in Turkey, which was associated with ancient Panormos, the port of Didyma.

name: Monte Stallone catacomb (Formello)
description: A Christian catacomb at Monte Stallone near Formello (Italy) was discovered in the 1960s.

name: Teleutskiy Vzvoz-I
description: Mound site on the Ob River in Altai Kray, Russia with burials from the Early Bronze Age Elunin culture.

name: Via Rovigno d'Istria catacombs
description: A small catacomb on two levels was discovered excavated in the tuff bedrock in 1953-1954 near the tract of the modern Via Prenestina in the Via Rovigno d'Istria.

name: Via Taranto columbaria
description: Two early imperial columbaria were discovered in 1932 along the Via Taranto.

name: Quleh
description: Burial site dated to the Chalcolithic period.

name: Archaeological area of Carife
description: An ancient necropolis at Carife (Campania) with more than 100 Samnite tombs.

name: Xixia Imperial Tombs
description: The Western Xia mausoleums are located in northwestern China in the Ningxia Hui Autonomous Region. This UNESCO World Heritage Site includes nine imperial mausoleums and 250 tombs of imperial relatives and officials of China's Western Xia dynasty.

name: Ilıca (Ayas: cemetery)
description: Cemetery site northwest of Ankara containing burials dated to the Hittite period (16th century BCE). The modern neighborhood of Ilıca is in the Ayas district of Turkey's Ankara province.

name: Cemetery of the Garamantes
description: Unnamed cemetery in the el-Agial escarpment

description: Unnamed cemetery in the el-Agial escarpment

name: Unnamed Place
description: An unnamed place of Tripolitana

name: Li Muri necropolis
description: A necropolis with four dolmens surrounded by small kerbstone circles.

name: Tomb of Cossinia
description: A Roman tomb on the right bank of the Anio discovered in 1929. The tomb dates to the first century A.D. and is associated with Cossinia, a priestess of the cult of Vesta.

name: Pyramid of Amenemhat I
description: The Pyramid of Amenemhat I is an ancient Egyptian pyramid located at Lisht. It was built for Pharaoh Amenemhat I, the founder of the Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt.

name: Tomba del Peccato
description: The so-called Tomba del Peccato has been a well-known rock-cut tomb in the hinterland of Falerii Novi since the nineteenth century. The tomb collapsed following heavy rains in 1991 and is in a ruinous condition.

name: Costa Bellene/Ballene (Capo Don?)
description: A Roman mansio on the via Iulia Augusta. An Early Christian complex (basilica, baptistery, cemetery) developed nearby and lasted until the Middle Ages.

name: Panagitsa
description: Abundant archaeological evidence at modern Panagitsa in the modern Greek municipal unit of Elatia attests to an ancient settlement and associated cemetery in use from the Geometric period through at least Hellenistic times. Its ancient name is uncertain; various suggestions have been made in the literature.

name: Pompey
description: Pompey (Meurthe-et-Moselle, France) produced archaeological evidence of a Roman productive site located near the junction of the Meurthe and Moselle rivers. This site was discovered in 1962 and has been described as a "villa rustica". There is evidence of baths and painted wall plaster. There is evidence for pottery production at the site, along with agricultural activity. There is a cremation necropolis of the second and third centuries CE described as containing "poor" finds.

name: Tomb of the Seven against Thebes at Eleusis
description: According to Pausanias, the Seven against Thebes were buried at Eleusis, at the road to Megara, after the sanctuary of Metaneira. The location of this monument is unknown today.

name: Porta Mediana necropolis
description: Porta Mediana necropolis is a necropolis at Cumae containing some 70 tombs, dating from the fourth century BCE to the fifth century CE.

name: Ponsas
description: The Chateau de Fontager (13th century) is the site of the discovery of an ancient necropolis, including Early Christian tombs as well as pre-Christian tombs.

name: Derveni tombs
description: Macedonian tombs and Hellenistic chamber tombs where the Derveni krater and Derveni papyri were discovered.

name: Prinias (Patela)
description: An Iron Age settlement on the Patela plateau north of the modern village of Prinias; its ancient name is uncertain. The site is notable for its occupation from the end of the Bronze Age through to the Archaic period, as well as for the monumental architecture and Orientalizing sculpture of its Buildings ('Temples') A and B.

name: Moria
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 D3 Moria

name: Peqi'in Cave
description: Cave located near the modern village of al-Buqei'a, used as a burial site in the late Chalcolithic period.

name: Pianello di Genga
description: Pianello di Genga, located in Italy's Ancona province, was the site of a Final Bronze Age settlement, as attested by an extensive necropolis.

name: Pyramid of Kenchreai
description: A pyramidal structure located near the modern village of Hellenikon in Greece, whose ancient name is unknown. It is variously interpreted as one of several mass graves mentioned by Pausanias or as a fort or guard tower for a farm or other installation. Possibly of classical date.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 G3 unnamed unlabeled ((1 bath, 1 cemetery))

name: Mausoleum of Diocletian
description: A mausoleum built to entomb Diocletian at his retirement palace that was constructed around 295 CE and is located in modern day Split, Croatia.

name: Tomb Garden of Claudia Semne
description: Latin inscriptions unearthed in 1792 between the second and third mile of the Via Appia near S. Sebastiano identify the tomb garden complex of one Claudia Semne.

name: Carrowkeel Megalithic Cemetery
description: A Neolithic cemetery dated to 3400 to 3100 BC. Fourteen passage tombs have been identified.

name: Dólmenes de Antequera
description: A Chalcolithic necropolis located in Antequera, province of Málaga, Andalusia, Spain. The Antequera Dolmens Site was enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2016.

name: Unstan Chambered Cairn
description: A Neolithic chambered cairn dating to the fourth and third millennia BC.

name: Church and early Christian necropolis at Vaste
description: An early Christian complex that included a cult building with several construction phases and a vast cemetery.

name: Nuraghe Succoronis
description: A single-tower nuraghe located near Macomer, Sardinia.

name: The Tomb of Protesilaus
description: Tomb of the mythic figure Protesilaus, first to die in the Trojan War. According to ancient testimony, the tomb was located on the Chersonesus, near Elaeus, its precise location is not known today.
